representative.rationale: A shipped, documented tool unconditionally archives 91-day-old mail from lawyers/banks/.gov/self, directly violating the product's stated protected-sender guarantee with no gate or audit backstop; data-loss class, critical confirmed.

representative.location: GET /v1/audit/{run_id} lines 281-305; cross-ref api/receipts.py sign()/get_receipt() lines 66-142
representative.title: Live /v1/audit/{run_id} fabricates an UNSIGNED, hardcoded receipt — the demo's 'tamper-evident signed receipt' headline trust artifact is fake
representative.evidence: worker.mjs 281-305 returns a fixed body {run_id, receipt:`Signed receipt for ${runId}`, audit:{total:3,protected_held:2,archived:1,...}} with NO signature/signed_body/algorithm. api/receipts.py get_receipt (lines 114-142) returns {signed_body, signature, algorithm:'HMAC-SHA256', verify:'...', created_at} and 404s on missing receipts (123-124); sign() (66-68) is real HMAC-SHA256. agent.json advertises 'audit_receipt: independent, HMAC-signed, re-derivable' and worker /llms.txt line 315 advertises /v1/audit/{run_id}.
representative.rationale: The worker's headline 'signed receipt' is a literal label string with zero cryptographic content; an auditing agent has nothing to verify. Directly contradicts the advertised trust artifact.

representative.title: Triage execution path NEVER enforces monthly_run_cap, run_credits, or plan entitlements — paid limits and purchased credits are sold but unenforced
representative.evidence: service.run_triage(service.py:70-135) takes provider/query/limit/dry_run/etc and NO account_id/api_key; it builds a provider, runs the labeler, asserts no gate violations, and returns — never touching credits/caps. app._run (app.py:97-130) and mcp_server/server.py:_triage (129-146) both call service.run_triage with the same signature; neither passes nor looks up an account. grep confirms enforcement primitives have no live callers: `consume_credit` appears only at api/store.py:235 (def) and in tests/test_store.py; `entitlements_for` appears only at api/plans.py:175 (def) — zero callers in api/, mcp_server/, acp/; `monthly_run_cap` is referenced only for display (scripts/gen_commerce_artifacts.py:46, api/plans.py catalog rows 37/58/74/92/111/189) and never as a runtime check. Conversely credits ARE sold/minted: acp/router.py:290 calls store.fulfill_once -> api/store.py:365/384 UPDATE accounts SET run_credits = run_credits + ?. So real-money ACP credit packs add run_credits but the consuming triage loop never debits them and never enforces the Free 50 / Pro 5000 caps.
representative.rationale: Verified: all three triage entry points lack any account lookup, credit debit, or run-cap check; consume_credit/entitlements_for have no production callers while fulfill_once does mint paid credits — the metered/credit/cap monetization model is genuinely unenforced (unlimited free triage; purchased credits never consumed).

representative.title: Broad except swallows all errors (auth, network, 5xx) and degrades to empty/false, hiding real failures
representative.evidence: list_messages (438-440) returns ListMessagesResult(messages=[]) on ANY Exception; cli.py (192-194) treats empty messages as 'No more messages found' and breaks the loop. apply_category (349-350) bare-excepts to current_cats=[]. _init_folder_cache (261) and _init_category_cache (284) log a warning and continue with empty caches. All confirmed verbatim.
representative.rationale: Confirmed: a mid-pagination 401/429/5xx is swallowed into an empty result that the CLI reads as successful completion — silent early termination on a bulk job. High kept.

representative.location: apply_label 279, remove_label 303, star 331, unstar 340, mark_read 364, mark_unread 373, archive 321
representative.title: imaplib uid(STORE/...) errors are exceptions OR ('NO', ...) tuples; success returned True without checking response status
representative.evidence: Verified imaplib.IMAP4._command_complete raises self.error ONLY on typ=='BAD'; a typ=='NO' (label missing, permission, quota, invalid flag) returns ('NO',[...]) without raising. Lines 279-280/303-304/331-332/340-341/364-365/373-374 each call uid('STORE',...) then unconditionally `return True`, catching only exceptions. base.apply_actions (base.py:344-346) records applied_labels from this True return into the audit. The COPY branch (288-289) and ensure_label_exists DO check res, proving the asymmetry.
representative.rationale: Server NO rejections are silently reported as success; primary trust-surface defect (label add) — confirmed against real imaplib semantics.

representative.title: ACP order-receipt save is NOT best-effort (router calls store.save_receipt directly), so a receipts-table write failure 500s AFTER credit is applied
representative.evidence: router.py complete_session: store.fulfill_once(...) commits the credit at line 290; THEN order_id minted (296), receipts.sign() (312) and store.save_receipt() (313-317) are called with NO try/except. receipts.persist() (88-108) deliberately wraps save_receipt in 'except Exception' (106) — the ACP path bypasses persist() entirely. So a receipts-table write failure after fulfill_once raises an unhandled 500 with credit already applied, receipt missing, and idempotency not yet completed (_complete_idempotency is at 338, after save_receipt).
representative.rationale: Confirmed and corroborated: the two receipt-write call sites have opposite error semantics, and the unprotected one is on the money path post-credit-commit. High severity stands — inconsistent committed state on a paid transaction.

representative.title: COPY+DELETE fallback expunges the ENTIRE mailbox per message, deleting any other \Deleted-flagged messages
representative.evidence: Line 76 calls `imap.expunge()`; Python's imaplib signature is `IMAP4.expunge(self)` (verified, no UID arg) -> mailbox-wide EXPUNGE that purges ALL \Deleted-flagged messages in the selected INBOX, not just this uid. Called once per archivable uid inside the apply loop (lines 161-165), so O(n) full expunges. The COPY+STORE+expunge fallback (72-77) fires only when UID MOVE returns non-OK or raises imaplib.IMAP4.error (67-71).
representative.rationale: Genuine unscoped-EXPUNGE data-loss path; downgraded critical->high because it only triggers when the server lacks RFC 6851 MOVE (iCloud, the primary target, supports MOVE) AND unrelated \Deleted mail already exists in INBOX.

representative.title: Priority ties resolved by dict-insertion order silently misroute critical mail (gov mail with 'unsubscribe'/'newsletter' -> Marketing/Reference)
representative.evidence: Reproduced: categorize_with_tier('Benefits <noreply@ssa.gov>','newsletter: ... unsubscribe here') -> label=Marketing, tier=4. Priority histogram confirms 17 is shared by Marketing, Tech/Storage, Personal/Government in that insertion order; _find_best_label line 1033 uses strict '<' so the first-inserted same-priority rule wins. Marketing is defined at line 429, Personal/Government at 468 (Marketing wins the tie).
representative.rationale: Real defect: a gov email containing any bulk-mail keyword is demoted from Critical(tier1) to Reference(tier4) by dict-insertion order alone. Sub-claim 'priority 9 Tech/Security beats Personal/Health' and 'priority 8 Finance/Payments beats Finance/Tax' confirmed by histogram (ties exist).

representative.title: Gmail provider never populates EmailMessage.date, so escalate/pending age logic is a silent no-op
representative.evidence: All three EmailMessage constructions (gmail.py:157-161, 204-211, 286-293) omit date=, which defaults to None (core/models.py:48). The metadata fetches request metadataHeaders=["From","Subject"] (lines 177, 243) — no Date header retrieved. calculate_email_age_hours(None) returns 0 (core/rules.py: 'if email_date is None: return 0'). escalate_by_age then hits 'if email_age_hours < 24: should_escalate=False'. cli.py:883 calls calculate_email_age_hours(msg.date) and cli.py:667 builds the pending age column from msg.date.
representative.rationale: Confirmed end-to-end: Gmail msg.date is always None, so escalate is a silent no-op and pending age always reports 0h.

representative.title: Non-UTF-8 local protected_senders config crashes the module at import time, disabling the entire safety gate
representative.evidence: Reproduced: with PROTECTED_SENDERS_FILE pointing at a file containing byte 0xff, `import core.rules` raises UnicodeDecodeError ('utf-8' codec can't decode byte 0xff). Line 600 opens with encoding='utf-8'; line 611 only catches (FileNotFoundError, OSError). UnicodeDecodeError is a ValueError subclass, not caught. _load_local_protected runs at import (line 616), so the whole module fails to import.
representative.rationale: Docstring promises 'Never raises.' A user-supplied non-UTF-8 protected_senders config takes down the entire safety gate and every consumer at import. Fix: catch UnicodeDecodeError / errors='replace' / broaden except.

representative.title: Gmail batchModify is treated as all-or-nothing, but invalid ids within a chunk fail the whole call, marking valid messages in the chunk as errored
representative.evidence: Lines 452-472: each chunk of up to BATCH_MODIFY_SIZE=1000 ids is one batchModify call. The except at 469-472 increments error_count by len(chunk) and leaves `succeeded` unchanged for the whole chunk. There is no per-id isolation or smaller-chunk fallback. Gmail returns a single 400 for the entire batchModify if any id is invalid, so one bad id strands up to 999 valid messages as error/unapplied; audit (482-495) then records labels_added=[] for all of them.
representative.rationale: Confirmed poison-pill: a single invalid id fails the whole 1000-id chunk with no isolation.

representative.title: Pagination is broken: loops re-issue the same query and break after one full page instead of paging through nextPageToken
representative.evidence: Lines 92-96: `while True: results = service.users().messages().list(userId='me', q=query, maxResults=1000).execute()` with NO pageToken anywhere (grep for nextPageToken/pageToken returns nothing). Lines 112-115: `if not archivable: if len(ids) < 1000: break; continue`. When a full page of 1000 IDs is returned and every sender is_protected (archivable empty), no batchModify runs (line 122 is skipped), the INBOX label is not removed, so the next list() returns the identical 1000 messages and the loop `continue`s forever.
representative.rationale: The all-protected full-page path is a genuine non-terminating loop because progress depends solely on batchModify shrinking the result set, which never happens when archivable is empty.

representative.title: Live demo's protected-sender set is 3 domains vs the engine's 14+ — IRS/SSA/DocuSign/Apple/Google/Anthropic/1Password-equivalents all UNprotected on the shipped site
representative.evidence: worker.mjs line 1: PROTECTED = new Set(["courts.ca.gov","chase.com","1password.com"]) and senderCheck (82-169) only protects courts.ca.gov/.gov, chase.com, 1password.com. core/rules.py EXAMPLE_PROTECTED_SENDERS (lines 560-580) lists docusign.net, irs.gov, ssa.gov, studentaid.gov, login.gov, apple.com, appleid.com, google.com, accounts.google.com, anthropic.com, 1password.com, meta.com, facebookmail.com, chase.com + synthetic placeholders + a merged local file. web/index.html:354 promises 'court, bank, gov, account, and client mail' protection.
representative.rationale: The live worker's protection coverage (3 domains + bare .gov) is materially narrower than the engine's 14+ generic defaults; apple.com / accounts.google.com / docusign.net / irs.gov etc. all return 'Can move' on the shipped demo while marketing promises they are held.

representative.title: irs.gov / ssa.gov / studentaid.gov never categorize as Personal/Government (Critical) because lower-priority earlier rules match first
representative.evidence: Reproduced exactly: categorize_with_tier('IRS <noreply@irs.gov>','Your tax refund status') -> label=Finance/Tax, tier=2 (NOT Personal/Government tier 1). irs.gov is listed both in Finance/Tax (priority 8, line 203) and Personal/Government (priority 17, line 476); the lower priority number (8) wins via line 1033.
representative.rationale: Distinct from U064: this is lower-priority-rule-wins (8<17), not a same-priority tie. Government mail from irs.gov silently downgraded from Critical to Important; the tier table promises Critical.

representative.title: AppleScript injection: message_id and label interpolated unescaped into osascript source
representative.evidence: Every mutating method builds AppleScript via f-string with zero escaping: line 213/265/297/313/362/378 `first message whose id is {message_id}` (unquoted), line 266 `mailbox "{label}"`, line 342 `make new mailbox with properties {{name:"{label}"}}`, line 133/261/332/409 `of account "{self.account}"`. No escape helper exists anywhere in the module (full file read).
representative.rationale: Genuine AppleScript-injection sink; downgraded from high because all sources (message_id from Mail.app's own data, label from static rules/config, account from CLI --account) are locally-controlled, not remote/untrusted input.

representative.title: MCP triage tool has no auth/account/metering — unlimited free live triage for any connected agent
representative.evidence: mcp_server/server.py imports only `api.service` (line 34) and `api.schemas` (line 35) — never `api.store`. _triage (129-146) calls service.run_triage with provider/query/limit/dry_run/remove_label/tier_routing/vip_only and NO account/api_key. grep confirms entitlements_for and consume_credit have ZERO runtime callers (only tests + the credit-pack fulfillment in acp/router.py). plans.py:130-139 advertises the metered '$0.01/triage run' add-on and Business-tier MCP access; meter_event_name='triage_run' is defined but emitted nowhere. The only guard on the surface is _transport_security() DNS-rebinding.
representative.rationale: Real: no auth/credit/cap/meter on the MCP triage path. But the SAME gap exists on the HTTP path (api/app.py _run never checks credits/caps either), so this is system-wide, not an MCP-unique unlimited-free-triage hole; severity downgraded from high to medium accordingly.

representative.title: Unbounded outer while-loop can run forever / loop indefinitely when domains keep mapping to the same Misc/Other
representative.evidence: Line 100 `while True:` breaks only at lines 111-113 when source label empty. Line 133 fetches headers only for `messages[:100]`. Lines 190-196: `if moves_performed == 0:` block ends in bare `pass` with the literal comment 'we might loop forever.' (line 192). Only `time.sleep(2)` (line 199) between iterations. If bulk-move query yields nothing (broken `from:` match) or target label missing (continue at 148-150), moves_performed stays 0 and the still-non-empty bucket is re-sampled.
representative.rationale: Real no-progress / runaway-loop hazard, self-admitted in code; severity downgraded to medium since it is a LEGACY/STANDALONE script not chained into run_automation (grep shows no automation reference) and 500-msg sampling usually does produce moves.

representative.title: Non-atomic state-file write can corrupt resume state on crash
representative.evidence: core/state.py:92-93 `with open(self.filename, "w") as f: json.dump(self.state, f, indent=2)` — truncate-in-place, no temp+os.replace. save() is called repeatedly mid-run (cli.py:300/319/323, gmail_labeler.py:308/322/332/335). _load (52-56) silently resets to default on JSONDecodeError, so a torn write loses the resume token.
representative.rationale: Non-atomic write of a crash-recovery state file is a real defect; downgraded from high to medium because blast radius is at-most a re-run from scratch (idempotent labeling), not data loss, and the crash window is narrow.

representative.title: set -u aborts iCloud step (and whole script) if ICLOUD_IMAP_* env vars are unset
representative.evidence: run_automation.sh:5 `set -euo pipefail`; lines 47-49 expand `$ICLOUD_IMAP_HOST/USER/PASS` with no `:-` defaults. Reproduced under bash: `set -euo pipefail; IMAP_PASS="$ICLOUD_IMAP_PASS" /bin/echo ran || echo guarded-fail` -> `bash: ICLOUD_IMAP_PASS: unbound variable`, the `||` guard did NOT fire, and the following `echo` (banner equivalent) never ran (outer exit=1). Decisively, the real sourced file ~/.config/op/mail_automation.env.op.sh exports ICLOUD_IMAP_USER/ICLOUD_IMAP_PASS ONLY inside an `if command -v op ...; then ... fi` block (lines 19-29), so on any machine without the op CLI those vars are genuinely unset, triggering exactly this abort.
representative.rationale: set -u aborts on the unguarded ICLOUD_* expansion before the || guard, killing the iCloud step and the completion banner; the op-absent code path in the real env file makes the unset condition concretely reachable.

representative.title: Secret passed as 1Password CLI argument is exposed in process listing / shell history
representative.evidence: Line 111: cmd = ["op", "item", "edit", item, f"{field}={value}"] — the secret `value` is an argv element passed to subprocess.run (lines 33-38, no shell). The identical pattern exists in gmail_auth.py:57 (_op_item_edit, called by store_token_info at line 154, the live Gmail token write-back). sensitive=True at line 117 only suppresses stderr in error text (line 43-44), it does not affect argv.
representative.rationale: Plaintext token JSON is on the `op` process argv, visible via ps/proc to any local user for the subprocess lifetime; real for the write-back path.

representative.title: Docker build copies entire repo (no .dockerignore) — bakes data/app.db (API keys, Stripe customer ids) and local secrets into the image
representative.evidence: Dockerfile line 12 is `COPY . .` with no .dockerignore (`ls .dockerignore` -> No such file or directory). The build context working tree contains gitignored sensitive files that COPY . . would bake into image layers: data/ (state_store.db dir present; store.py:38 documents data/app.db as holding 'customer ids and api keys'), config/protected_senders.local.txt (PII, gitignored per .gitignore comment 'real lawyer/banks/gov/self'), and multiple *.log files (gmail_labeler.log 3.8MB, auto_drain.log/auto_drain_run.log ~1MB each). git check-ignore confirms data/state_store.db, gmail_labeler.log, config/protected_senders.local.txt are all gitignored — so git-tracking protects the repo but Docker COPY ignores .gitignore entirely.
representative.rationale: Accurate: no .dockerignore, COPY . ., and the named secret/PII files genuinely exist in the build context; high severity holds for an autoDeploy:true docker service shipping API keys/PII into registry layers.

representative.title: Bulk-sender router moves protected mail with no protected-sender gate (and unanchored substring match)
representative.evidence: route_bulk_senders.applescript line 9: `if theSender contains ("@" & dom) or theSender ends with dom then` -> line 28 `move msg to bulkMailbox`. There is no protected-sender check anywhere in the 33-line file (grep for protected/is_protected returns nothing). The `ends with dom` branch has no leading-dot boundary, so dom='mailchimp.com' matches a sender ending in 'notmailchimp.com'. Contrast core/rules.py:659-663 `_domain_matches` which intentionally does `domain == entry or domain.endswith('.' + entry)` to kill substring embeds, and core/audit.py:1-33 which states the headline guarantee that a protected sender is NEVER moved out of the inbox. This standalone AppleScript bypasses both the gate and the audit.
representative.rationale: Both claimed defects are real in the current code: no protected-sender gate and an unanchored `ends with` substring match that moves mail out of the inbox; the Python guarantees it violates are present as cited.

representative.location: _get_session (196-211) + _api_get/_api_post/_api_patch (213-232); token acquired once in connect() at 234-239
representative.title: Access token never refreshed mid-run; long sweeps fail with 401 once the token expires
representative.evidence: connect() (236) sets self._access_token = self._acquire_token() once. _get_session() (198) short-circuits `if self._session: return self._session`, so the Bearer header (208) is baked in only at first creation and never refreshed. _api_get/post/patch (213-232) call raise_for_status() with no 401-aware refresh/retry. No acquire_token_silent between batches. There is also no in-process token-expiry check.
representative.rationale: Real: a long single-connection sweep will 401 after token TTL with no refresh path; downgraded to medium because the main label loop sleeps 1s/batch and a typical run completes well within a ~60-90min token, so impact is limited to very large mailboxes.

representative.title: Idempotency key is not namespaced by api_key (Authorization), enabling cross-account replay / confused-deputy disclosure
representative.evidence: _begin_idempotency calls store.idempotency_begin(ctx.idempotency_key, scope, _request_hash(raw)) (router.py:104-106). store.py:313-353 keys the idempotency_keys table on `key` (PRIMARY KEY) with scope+request_hash only — api_key is never part of the key or stored. On a same-key/same-body collision from a different caller, store.py:350-353 returns the first caller's response_json verbatim, which (after completion) contains buyer PII and the order permalink (router.py:318-322).
representative.rationale: Confirmed cross-account replay/disclosure: idempotency scope omits the credential. Distinct mechanism (replay store) from the bearer-validation cluster.

representative.title: IMAP search passes raw query string with charset=None; Gmail-style queries (label:, has:) are NOT valid IMAP SEARCH and will error or mis-search
representative.evidence: Line 184: `self._connection.uid("search", None, query)` forwards `query` verbatim. cli.py:1014 sets `--query` default="has:nouserlabels" for ALL providers (no per-provider override on the label path), and cli.py:442 passes args.query into run_labeler → list_messages. `has:`/`label:` are not valid IMAP SEARCH keys; line 185-186 raises RuntimeError on a server NO. The report path (cli.py:480-484) guards `f"label:{label}"` behind `if args.provider == 'gmail'`, but the label/escalate/summary paths do not — so the default query reaches IMAP raw.
representative.rationale: Gmail query dialect reaches IMAP SEARCH verbatim with no translation; common-path failure on the default label command.

representative.title: Unguarded int() cast on MAIL_AUTO_BATCH_SIZE crashes entire CLI/config load at startup
representative.evidence: Lines 272-273: `if os.getenv(f"{prefix}BATCH_SIZE"): config.batch_size = int(os.getenv(f"{prefix}BATCH_SIZE"))` — bare int() with no try/except. DRY_RUN (271) and IMAP_GMAIL_EXTENSIONS (287) use tolerant string-membership parsing; this is the lone unguarded numeric cast. load_config() is called at cli.py:422,523,718,828, so a non-int env value (e.g. '100x') raises ValueError before any command runs.
representative.rationale: Real unguarded cast that aborts every CLI command, but severity 'high' is inflated — config.batch_size is never actually consumed (only read in tests), so blast radius is a startup crash on a deliberate typo, not data loss; downgrade to medium.

representative.title: Protected-sender disposition over-reported as protected_held even when message left the inbox (count vs violation mismatch)
representative.evidence: Lines 181-184: `if is_protected: disposition = PROTECTED_HELD; if left_inbox: self.violations.append(message_id)`. counts[PROTECTED_HELD] is incremented (line 194) even for a violated message. summary() (245) reports protected_held from that count, and receipt_line (267) labels it 'protected held in inbox'. There is no distinct 'protected_violated' disposition. tests/test_audit.py:144 confirms a violated protected message returns disposition PROTECTED_HELD.
representative.rationale: Real internal contradiction: the held count and the persisted disposition both claim 'held in inbox' for a message recorded as having left it; only the parallel violations list signals the breach. Root cause is the disposition assignment at line 182.

representative.title: Charge treats only status=='succeeded' as ok, dropping 'requires_action'/'processing' into a permanent failure that strands a real payment
representative.evidence: payment.py:92-97 returns ok=True only when getattr(intent,'status')=='succeeded'; 'processing'/'requires_action' fall to the ok=False/error branch. router.py:267-280 then re-persists status=STATUS_READY and returns 402. Grep confirms NO payment_intent.* webhook anywhere: billing.py webhook (lines 225-272) handles only checkout.session.completed / subscription / invoice events. So a charge that settles asynchronously at Stripe will never trigger fulfillment (router fulfills only on the synchronous result.ok==True path, 290-317).
representative.rationale: Real gap: async-settled ACP PaymentIntent has no reconciliation path, so a 'processing' charge that later succeeds credits no runs. Severity downgraded from high to medium: this SPT/card-only flow with confirm=True and automatic capture almost always returns 'succeeded' synchronously; 'processing'/'requires_action' is the uncommon branch.

representative.location: main, slice line 144: uids = uids[max(0, len(uids) - args.start - args.limit) : len(uids) - args.start]
representative.title: Paging slice computes a negative end index when start > len(uids), selecting wrong (oldest) messages
representative.evidence: Reproduced exactly: python3 with uids=range(10), start=15, limit=5 -> slice end index = len(uids)-start = -5 (negative), so the slice [max(0,-10):-5] = uids[0:-5] = [0,1,2,3,4] (the 5 OLDEST messages) instead of []. Line 144 uses an unclamped end index.
representative.rationale: Real off-by-paging bug: paging past the end silently selects oldest messages instead of an empty page; end should be max(0, len(uids)-args.start). Blast radius limited (only when start>len and DRY_RUN unset; default DRY_RUN=1), so medium is fair.

representative.title: Folder lookup fallback can silently return the label string instead of a real folder ID, causing wrong-destination move
representative.evidence: Line 651 `return self._folder_cache.get(label, label)` returns the raw label string when the cache miss occurs. The inner fallback (644-647) only updates the cache when result.get('value') is truthy; an empty value list leaves parent_id/cache unchanged with no exception. apply_label (527-530) then POSTs /move with destinationId=<label string>.
representative.rationale: Confirmed contract violation (returns label not folder id); blast radius bounded because Graph /move with a bogus destinationId 400s and is caught at 535 returning False (fail-ish-safe), so wrong-folder move requires the label to collide with a well-known folder id — narrow. Medium.

representative.title: calculate_email_age_hours returns 0 for None date, conflating 'unknown age' with 'brand new' and masking the provider gap
representative.evidence: Lines 1173-1174 return 0 when email_date is None. Confirmed gmail.py/imap.py/mailapp.py never set date= on EmailMessage (grep: only outlook.py sets date= at lines 464,515; models.py:48 defaults date=None). cli.py:667/883 call calculate_email_age_hours(msg.date); escalate_by_age line 1122 takes the <24h no-escalation branch for age 0.
representative.rationale: Real root-cause enabler: age-0 default silently disables age-based escalation on 3 of 4 providers with no signal. Medium is appropriate (feature degradation, not a crash; escalation is an opt-in triage feature).

representative.location: feed (lines 67-69) -> build_feed (33-64); also router.py _links (125-130) and permalink_url (321)
representative.title: Host header reflected into absolute feed/checkout/seller URLs (Host-header injection / cache-poisoning of the agent discovery surface)
representative.evidence: feed.py:69 `JSONResponse(build_feed(str(request.base_url)))`; build_feed embeds base into url/image_url (45-47), seller_url (55), checkout_url (62). request.base_url is derived from the client Host header in Starlette. router.py:126 `_links` and :321 `permalink_url` also build from request.base_url. No env-configured trusted origin is used at request time.
representative.rationale: Genuine Host-header reflection into the agent-facing checkout/seller URLs; exploitability depends on a poisonable shared cache or a proxy that trusts client Host, so medium (not high) is right.

representative.location: build_line_items lines 86-96 (base = int(pack['amount_cents']); subtotal = base * qty; total_runs += int(pack['runs']) * qty)
representative.title: Line-item subtotal and total_runs are unbounded int*qty products with no per-session amount cap before charging
representative.evidence: models.py:87 subtotal=base*qty, :96 total_runs+=runs*qty. Item.quantity is bounded le=1000 (line 27) but CheckoutCreate.items (line 43) has no max_items. router.py:257 amount=grand_total(current['line_items']) is passed verbatim to payment.get_payment_client().charge(amount=...) (router.py:262-266). No monetary ceiling exists in models.py, router.py, or payment.py. payment.py:89-91 returns generic 'charge failed' on Stripe rejection.
representative.rationale: Real: there is genuinely no aggregate amount cap before the charge, and Stripe rejection surfaces as a generic error — but blast radius is limited (auth-gated endpoint, fail-closed charge, Stripe enforces its own ceiling), so medium is inflated.

representative.title: Metered per-run billing advertised but no Stripe Meter event is ever emitted
representative.evidence: grep -rn 'meter_event|triage_run|meter_events|billing.meter' --include='*.py' . returns a single hit: the literal 'meter_event_name': 'triage_run' string at plans.py:136. No stripe.billing.meter_events / meter reporting call exists anywhere (service.py, app.py, billing.py, mcp_server). The $0.01/run metered path is declared but no usage is ever reported to Stripe.
representative.rationale: Metered revenue mechanism is unimplemented — runs are never counted or reported to a Stripe meter. Corroborated flag echoed from slice.

representative.title: Success-charge fake ignores the amount argument, so no test asserts the charged amount equals grand_total / equals the credited runs' price
representative.evidence: router.py:257 amount = models.grand_total(current['line_items']); router.py:262-266 passes that amount to charge(); the receipt summary carries it (router.py:305-306 'amount': amount). The test asserts run_credits==200 (line 133) and runs_credited==200 (line 138) but never 'amount'. Critically, pack_100 has amount_cents==100 AND runs==100 (api/plans.py:144), so the credited count and charged amount are numerically identical for this SKU — asserting one cannot pin the other. A per-run-vs-pack-total or qty-not-multiplied bug on the money axis would pass green.
representative.rationale: Confirmed money-path coverage gap: the only charged-amount evidence in the test is numerically aliased to runs because pack price==runs, so amount is genuinely unverified.

representative.title: Manifest/llms.txt reflect the raw request base_url (Host header) — Host-header spoofing makes the self-describing surface advertise attacker-controlled URLs
representative.evidence: well_known.py:80,85 pass str(request.base_url) straight into the builders, which embed it as absolute checkout_url/mcp url/receipt_verification (lines 29,35-36,41-43,68,72). Grep for TrustedHost/allowed_hosts across api/ returns nothing; app.py (lines 56-165) installs NO middleware at all (no TrustedHostMiddleware, no CORS), and no configured PUBLIC_BASE_URL exists. Dockerfile:19 launches uvicorn with no --forwarded-allow-ips/proxy config. Starlette derives base_url from the Host header, so a forged Host makes the served manifest advertise attacker-controlled commerce/MCP/receipt URLs to trusting agents.
representative.rationale: Host-header reflection into a B2A discovery surface with zero host validation is present in current code; medium severity is justified for an agent-trusted checkout/receipt path.

representative.title: Ephemeral signing key regenerated per process; previously-signed receipts become permanently unverifiable after restart/across workers
representative.evidence: _signing_key() (44-57): when RECEIPT_SIGNING_KEY unset, _EPHEMERAL_KEY = secrets.token_bytes(32) per process, only logger.warning once. store.py docstring (21-24) explicitly states 'the API is a single process' — but a restart still rotates the key, so receipts persisted before restart (save_receipt wrote signature durably, store.py 287) can never re-verify. The MCP server advertises stateless_http (horizontal scaling), making cross-worker mismatch plausible.
representative.rationale: Real silent trust failure on the misconfigured (env-unset) production path; correctly described. Downgraded high->medium because it only manifests when the deploy secret is forgotten (the code warns loudly and the durable-key path is the documented production config).

representative.title: Plist read-modify-write not atomic; backup overwritten each run
representative.evidence: Line 103 `shutil.copy2(PLIST_PATH, BACKUP_PATH)` runs at the start of EVERY invocation, and BACKUP_PATH (line 18) is a fixed `.backup` suffix (not timestamped) — so a second run overwrites the prior backup. Lines 123-124 `with PLIST_PATH.open('wb') as fh: plistlib.dump(new_list, fh)` write directly onto the live plist with no temp-file + os.replace; grep confirms no tempfile/os.replace/dry_run anywhere in the file. A crash/Ctrl-C mid-dump truncates the live SyncedSmartMailboxes.plist, and the only backup may already be the corrupted copy.
representative.rationale: Both halves verified in current code: backup is clobbered each run AND the write is non-atomic onto a real Mail data file; this is the primary statement of the cluster.

representative.title: entitlements_for() is dead code — defined but never called by any request path
representative.evidence: grep -rn 'entitlements_for' --include='*.py' . returns exactly one hit: the definition at api/plans.py:175. No endpoint, service, MCP tool, ACP handler, or test references it. billing.py:248-256 (_handle_event) re-implements the active/canceled downgrade independently and never calls entitlements_for; the comment at billing.py:250 ('entitlements also enforce this') is aspirational.
representative.rationale: The function is genuinely uncalled by any request path; the entitlement-resolution layer has zero runtime effect. Severity downgraded from high to medium: it is unreachable governance code, not an active mischarge — no caps are advertised-but-bypassed because no caller ever invoked enforcement.

representative.title: Refresh catches only RefreshError; transient network errors crash and a post-refresh write-back failure is not isolated
representative.evidence: Lines 166-173: `try: creds.refresh(Request()); store_token_info(creds) except RefreshError: creds = None`. The single try wraps BOTH the refresh and the write-back, and the only handler is `except RefreshError` (imported line 11). google.auth.exceptions.TransportError (transient network failure during refresh) is a sibling of RefreshError, not a subclass, so it propagates uncaught. store_token_info (line 168) can raise RuntimeError ('Token storage not configured' line 147, or '1Password CLI failed/not found' from _run_op lines 30/33/35) AFTER a successful refresh — also not RefreshError — crashing the call and discarding the freshly-refreshed token.
representative.rationale: Both claimed escape paths are real: TransportError is not a RefreshError, and the write-back is not isolated from the refresh try; a post-refresh storage failure loses the refreshed token. Medium is fair.

representative.location: module docstring lines 5-11 and main() lines 96-109 (writes pricing.md, llms.txt, .well-known/agent.json, server.json — but NOT acp/product_feed.json)
representative.title: Commerce-artifact generator does not regenerate acp/product_feed.json — the feed is on a second, separate generation path and can drift
representative.evidence: main() (lines 97-108) writes exactly pricing.md, llms.txt, .well-known/agent.json, server.json; product_feed.json is absent. acp/feed.py has a SEPARATE entrypoint `if __name__ == '__main__': write_static_feed()` (lines 80-85) that regenerates acp/product_feed.json. Both surfaces read api.plans.CREDIT_PACKS (gen_pricing_md line 61; acp/feed.py build_feed line 38). DEPLOY.md:79 documents ONLY `PUBLIC_BASE_URL=... python scripts/gen_commerce_artifacts.py` — no mention of `python -m acp.feed`.
representative.rationale: Two independent generation paths derive from the same plans.py catalog; the documented single command refreshes 3 of 4 commerce artifacts and silently leaves product_feed.json stale, exactly defeating the docstring's 'never drift' claim.

representative.title: Tests exercise consume_credit/add_credits in isolation, masking that no production path calls them
representative.evidence: grep --include=*.py for consume_credit returns ONLY tests/test_store.py:39,42 plus the def at api/store.py:235 — zero production callers. add_credits is called only at tests/test_store.py:38 plus its def. No production route debits run_credits: grep for 'run_credits = run_credits -' / consume_credit / debit in api/ hits only store.py's consume_credit body; api/service.py and api/app.py contain no credit/consume/quota/cap logic. api/plans.py:189-192 (entitlements_for) merely *exposes* monthly_run_cap and run_credits but enforces nothing. grep of tests/ for monthly_run_cap|entitlements_for|429|quota = no hits. test_acp.py:133,153,173 do assert run_credits after ACP fulfill (via fulfill_once at store.py:384, a separate credit path), confirming only the credit-grant side is covered, never a debit.
representative.rationale: consume_credit/add_credits have no production callers; metering debit is never enforced or integration-tested, so the green unit tests mask a missing end-to-end metering path exactly as claimed.

representative.title: pending uses sender-side 'is:starred' query for gmail but 'isRead eq false' for outlook, then filters on msg.is_starred — outlook path yields zero pending items
representative.evidence: gmail query='is:starred' (640), outlook query='isRead eq false' (642), else query='' (644). Post-filter is `if msg.is_starred` for ALL providers (665). Verified outlook maps is_starred = (flag.flagStatus=='flagged') (providers/outlook.py:449,466,491,518), NOT isRead. So the outlook branch fetches UNREAD messages then keeps only FLAGGED ones — two orthogonal attributes — almost always yielding an empty/wrong pending list.
representative.rationale: Confirmed: outlook's query (unread) and post-filter (flagged) are uncorrelated, so cmd_pending --provider outlook discards nearly everything it fetched. The else-branch (non-gmail/non-outlook) fetches all then keeps starred, which matches the filter but ignores any 'due' semantics. Medium — pending is a user-facing triage command that is effectively broken for Outlook.

representative.title: OAuth refresh-token written to 1Password via command-line argument 'field=value' — secret exposed in process args
representative.evidence: Line 57: `cmd = ['op', 'item', 'edit', item, f'{field}={value}']` — the value (compact token JSON incl. long-lived refresh_token, built at store_token_info lines 151-152) is passed as a process argv, visible via ps/proc for the subprocess lifetime. Corroborated by the identical pattern at auth/onepassword.py:111 `cmd = ['op','item','edit', item, f'{field}={value}']`.
representative.rationale: Confirmed argv secret-exposure of a long-lived refresh token; corroboration flag holds (matching code in auth/onepassword.py).

representative.title: .gitignore does not cover *_state.json or mail_export.tsv, so runtime mailbox state will be committed on the next run
representative.evidence: git check-ignore returns NOT IGNORED for labeler_state.json, gmail_state.json, outlook_state.json, imap_state.json, mailapp_state.json, mail_export.tsv. .gitignore (37 lines) has no *_state.json or mail_export.tsv pattern. core/config.py:39/48/60/70 default each provider state_file to '<provider>_state.json'; gmail_labeler.py:40 STATE_FILE='labeler_state.json' -> StateManager(STATE_FILE) at line 71; core/state.py:92 opens self.filename in CWD (repo root) and json.dump writes it. labeler_state.json IS ALREADY TRACKED (git ls-files) and contains real per-category counts (total_processed=36470, real label histogram). mail_export.tsv is also tracked.
representative.rationale: Real coverage gap: provider state files write to repo root and are not git-ignored; labeler_state.json is already committed with real mailbox category counts. Severity adjusted high->medium: leaked data is aggregate label counts (counts, not addresses/subjects); the tracked mail_export.tsv currently holds only the header row (no sender PII rows committed yet), so present blast radius is metadata, not raw PII contents.

representative.location: lines 13-15 (mypy/types-requests commented out) vs .github/workflows/ci.yml:31 (pip install ... mypy)
representative.title: requirements.txt comments out mypy/types-requests as dev deps, but CI installs an unpinned mypy and runs it with no stub packages — type-check is dependency-incomplete
representative.evidence: requirements.txt:14-15 comment out `mypy>=1.0.0` and `types-requests>=2.28.0`. CI ci.yml:31 does `pip install pytest pytest-cov ruff mypy` (unpinned, no stubs). grep for types-requests across .github/workflows/ returns nothing (exit 1). mypy invoked with `--ignore-missing-imports` (ci.yml:52) under `set +e` (ci.yml:51), so the type check is advisory-only and stub-incomplete.
representative.rationale: All claims verified: unpinned mypy, no types-requests install path, defanged advisory run. Real but the type-check is non-gating and the gap is low-impact tooling completeness.

representative.title: Same hardcoded /Users/4jp absolute paths (ProgramArguments, WorkingDirectory, logs) — the plist deploy.sh actually installs
representative.evidence: Plist hardcodes /Users/4jp absolute paths: ProgramArguments (lines 9-10 /bin/bash + /Users/4jp/Code/organvm/universal-mail--automation/run_automation.sh), WorkingDirectory (line 13), StandardOutPath/StandardErrorPath (lines 15,17). deploy.sh:9-10,46-48 confirms it is the installed plist via literal `cp "$PLIST_SRC" "$PLIST_DEST"` with NO envsubst/sed templating; deploy.sh:60 bootstraps it. So on any machine whose home != /Users/4jp the LaunchAgent points at non-existent paths. CAVEAT: the cited provenance line is 98-99 (not 99 alone) and says 'dead Workspace/ paths' — but the current plist points at /Users/4jp/Code/organvm/... which DOES exist (ls -ld confirmed); so the provenance 'nonexistent paths' framing is stale w.r.t. the current corrected path. The portability defect itself is real.
representative.rationale: Real hardcoded-home portability defect confirmed against current source; severity downgraded high→medium because it is single-tenant tooling (engine documented as single-user, no onboarding) and the only consumer deploy.sh runs on the same user 4jp, so blast radius is a future-multi-user/different-home scenario, not a live break.

representative.title: ruff and mypy run advisory-only (set +e) — lint/type regressions can never fail CI
representative.evidence: ci.yml:35 ruff step `set +e`, captures ${PIPESTATUS[0]}, only writes a `::warning::` and step-summary on nonzero — step always exits 0. ci.yml:51 mypy step identical pattern. Comment at lines 46/62 explicitly says 'pytest remains the hard gate'. Combined with the 3.11/3.12-only matrix (U007), no automated gate catches a 3.9 typing/syntax regression.
representative.rationale: Accurate: both lint and type steps are non-blocking by design; pytest on two non-3.9 interpreters is the only hard gate.

representative.title: run_triage is the single triage chokepoint and accepts no caller identity — enforcement has no insertion point that is actually used
representative.evidence: run_triage signature (service.py:70-81) has only provider/query/limit/dry_run/remove_label/tier_routing/vip_only/audit_path/provider_factory — no account_id/api_key/entitlements. Both callers pass no identity: api/app.py:97-107 (_run) and mcp_server/server.py:129-138 (_triage). Repo-wide grep: consume_credit (defined api/store.py:235) and entitlements_for (defined api/plans.py:175) have ZERO runtime callers — only tests/test_store.py and the docs findings file reference them. service.py never imports api.store or api.plans.
representative.rationale: The structural premise is real: run_triage is the sole triage chokepoint and exposes no caller identity, so cap/credit checks cannot fire on the live path — the metering functions are genuinely dead. Medium is fair for the structural locus.

representative.title: batchModify and list calls have no error handling, no pagination, and an unchecked remove-label can erase intent
representative.evidence: Confirmed: run_sweep has NO try/except — `service.users().messages().list(...).execute()` (98-102) and `batchModify(...).execute()` (117) will raise HttpError on rate-limit/5xx and abort the sweep mid-rule with no state persistence (no StateManager used here at all). No backoff, unlike GmailProvider. Also confirmed line 76 `results['labels']` direct subscript -> KeyError risk. The bundled infinite-loop-on-None-remove sub-claim is the same overstated corner as U049/U050.
representative.rationale: The error-handling/no-backoff defect is real and accurately stated; the infinite-loop rider is overstated but does not negate the core finding — kept at medium (standalone legacy script, manual invocation).

representative.location: default --query 'has:nouserlabels' (label_parser line 1014) used for all providers
representative.title: Gmail-specific default query passed verbatim to IMAP/Outlook/Mail.app providers
representative.evidence: label_parser --query default='has:nouserlabels' (1014), help text 'default: has:nouserlabels for Gmail' (1015). cmd_label passes args.query straight into run_labeler (443) -> provider.list_messages(query=...) for ALL providers; get_provider/run_labeler do no per-provider remapping. The other commands (escalate 1089, summary 1131, vip 1176) default to '' which is provider-safe.
representative.rationale: Confirmed: `cli.py label --provider outlook` (no --query) sends the Gmail-only operator 'has:nouserlabels' to Outlook/IMAP/Mail.app, which is not a valid query for those backends. Likely matches nothing or errors per provider. Medium — affects the primary 'label' command's default behavior for non-Gmail providers.

representative.title: Offline sender-check fallback is fail-OPEN: only 3 hardcoded patterns are protected, contradicting the safety promise
representative.evidence: localSenderCheck (line 708) marks protected only for empty / .gov / chase.com / 1password.com; line 753 returns protected:false otherwise, so e.g. alerts@bankofamerica.com renders the green 'Can move' verdict (line 818). True. BUT this fallback ONLY fires when the fetch to /v1/senders/check fails (line 809: ok && data ? data : localSenderCheck). The sole documented/CI deployment (wrangler.toml main=worker.mjs, custom_domain uma.4444j99.dev) serves a same-origin API: worker.mjs senderCheck (lines 82-170) has the SAME protection set, so on the live demo the fetch succeeds and the JS fallback is never reached. API_BASE defaults to '' (no injection site found).
representative.rationale: Real fail-open in the offline mirror, but it is a latent fallback that does not execute on the actual worker-backed deployment; severity downgraded from high and scope corrected (not the live surface).

representative.title: CI never exercises the Python 3.9 (or 3.10) floor the codebase is architected around
representative.evidence: ci.yml:15 matrix is ["3.11","3.12"]; 3.9 floor is documented in CLAUDE.md:278 ('Python 3.9+'), Dockerfile:6-7 ('core stays 3.9-importable without it'), requirements-mcp.txt:2 ('mcp SDK requires >=3.10... core stays importable on 3.9+'), api/app.py:33-46 lazy MCP import 'core API stays importable on 3.9', tests/test_mcp.py:11 pytest.importorskip('mcp'). core/rules.py:1163 does use Optional["datetime"] (string-quoted forward ref, no `from __future__ import annotations`).
representative.rationale: Real gap: the advertised 3.9 (and 3.10) import path is never executed in CI; the matrix only runs 3.11/3.12. Severity trimmed to medium since the 3.9 'support' is a soft/secondary claim (the top README badge actually says 3.10+), not a hard product contract.

representative.title: Completed session's created_at preserved but data_json fully replaced each persist — concurrent failed and successful /complete race to last-writer-wins on the whole row including status
representative.evidence: save_session uses INSERT OR REPLACE keyed on id with no compare-and-set on status (store.py:407-413; created_at is preserved at 403-406 but the whole data_json/status/account_id are replaced). Two concurrent /complete calls (one success at 332-337, one failed-payment at 273-280) each call _persist; last writer wins the entire row. A failed-payment _persist (status=READY, account_id=None) committing after the success _persist reverts the row to READY, dropping order/account_id, while fulfill_once (already committed) keeps the credit — making the session re-completable with the order receipt orphaned.
representative.rationale: Mechanism confirmed exactly. Severity medium not high: requires a genuine concurrent success+failure race on the same session within the single-writer-lock window, and the re-completion is charge-deduped by Stripe; data corruption of the durable record is real but narrow.

representative.title: run_credits is a write-only balance — granted by ACP fulfillment and add_credits, never read for an access decision
representative.evidence: run_credits incremented by add_credits (227) and fulfill_once (384), surfaced by plans.entitlements_for (192) and plans.py:192 in get-account response. grep confirms entitlements_for has NO production caller (only def at plans.py:175 + comment). consume_credit uncalled (see U033). No code reads run_credits to gate a triage run.
representative.rationale: Confirmed write-only balance; this is the read-side counterpart to U033's write-side, both real. Kept distinct (read vs debit) at medium.

representative.title: Worker .gov protection uses endsWith('.gov') on the full From value, accepting spoofs the real API rejects
representative.evidence: Line 106 protects when value.includes('courts.ca.gov') || value.endsWith('.gov') on the full From string, not a parsed domain. 'courts.ca.gov.evil.com' contains 'courts.ca.gov' -> protected. 'attacker@evil.gov' ends with '.gov' -> protected. core/rules.py _gov_protected: domain.split('.')[-1]=='gov' on the recovered domain only ('irs.gov.attacker.com'->False, 'service.gov.uk'->False).
representative.rationale: Confirmed parsed-domain-vs-raw-string divergence for the .gov path. Over-protect direction (medium). The finding's own hedging text acknowledges some examples balance out; the substring courts.ca.gov.evil.com pass-through is the solid instance.

representative.location: Notification patterns lines 414-422 + Tech/Storage/Notification priority interplay
representative.title: Notification catch-all keywords 'notification'/'alert'/'reminder' shadow more specific tier-1 categories below them
representative.evidence: Reproduced: categorize_with_tier('news@x.com','Reminder: your passport photo discount') -> Notification tier 3. Notification priority 16 (line 424) < Personal/Government priority 17 (line 480), and Notification's bare r'reminder' (line 416) matches first, demoting government/passport mail to Delegate.
representative.rationale: Real and distinct from U064 (this is lower-priority-wins, 16<17, not a same-priority tie). Government/Critical mail with a 'reminder'/'notification' subject is silently shadowed to tier 3 Delegate. Medium: more impactful than the other low-severity regex noise since it demotes a Critical category.

representative.location: LABEL_RULES line 282; categorize_email line 364; label_all_unlabeled_emails lines 386,430
representative.title: Legacy catch-all label is 'Uncategorized' while core uses 'Misc/Other' (taxonomy divergence)
representative.evidence: Legacy line 282 defines catch-all 'Uncategorized' (priority 999); line 364 returns `best_match or "Uncategorized"`; lines 383-386 create+cache it and lines 430-432 apply it. core/rules.py line 501/1038 uses 'Misc/Other'. run_automation.sh:37 sweeps `--query "label:Misc/Other"` and bulk_sweeper.py SWEEP_RULES all query `label:Misc/Other`, so 'Uncategorized'-labeled mail is never re-swept. Split-brain confirmed.
representative.rationale: Real taxonomy divergence, but downgraded from medium to low: the legacy file is not wired into any automation/CLI; only a user manually running this specific file would create the orphaned 'Uncategorized' label.

representative.title: Double-escaped regex patterns can never match real email text
representative.evidence: Empirically verified: r"render\\.com" (file source, line 62) is the char sequence ['r','e','n','d','e','r','\\','\\','.','c','o','m'] and re.search returns None on 'render.com' but matches a literal backslash. AST-extracted categorize() routes render.com/pieces.app/meta.com/self.inc/builtin.com -> 'Uncategorized'. NUANCE: e.godaddy.com still -> 'Services/Domain' (bare 'godaddy' pattern at line 239 catches it; the dead e\.godaddy\.com is redundant, not impactful), and ibo.org has no fallback bare pattern so only a sender whose text has no other Marketing keyword lands in Uncategorized. Line cites are off by a few (meta is 78 not 79) but patterns correctly identified.
representative.rationale: Double-backslash patterns are genuinely dead for 5 of 7 senders; severity downgraded from high to medium because this is an unwired standalone legacy file (no .sh/.yaml/cli reference; only main() invokes it with max_emails=None) and is relabel-only/non-destructive, and 2 of the 7 cited patterns (godaddy, ibo) are either redundant or rarely impactful.

representative.title: labeler_state.json (real mailbox stats) is git-tracked in a public repo despite CLAUDE.md flagging *_state.json as runtime state to avoid
representative.evidence: `git ls-files labeler_state.json` returns the path (tracked). `git cat-file -p HEAD:labeler_state.json` shows total_processed=36470 and the per-category history (Misc/Other=35521, Tech/Security=372, Finance/Payments=302, Finance/Banking=151, Professional/Jobs=79, etc.) exactly as claimed. `git log --follow` traces it to the initial commit 00ff9d7, so the value is recoverable from history. `git check-ignore labeler_state.json` exits 1 (NOT ignored); .gitignore has no *_state.json entry. CLAUDE.md 'Files to Avoid Modifying' does list '*_state.json (runtime state for resumption)'. `gh repo view` confirms isPrivate=false (PUBLIC).
representative.rationale: Real runtime-state file with private mailbox volume/composition is tracked and publicly exposed since the first commit; severity downgraded high->medium because the leak is coarse metadata (counts, no addresses/subjects/bodies/credentials).

representative.location: lines 6-15 (packages[].registryType=pypi, identifier=universal-mail-automation, version 0.1.0); cross-ref pyproject.toml (whole file) and scripts/gen_commerce_artifacts.py gen_server_json lines 82-89
representative.title: server.json advertises a PyPI package that the repo cannot build or publish (no [project]/[build-system] in pyproject.toml)
representative.evidence: pyproject.toml is exactly 3 lines and contains ONLY [tool.pytest.ini_options] (testpaths/pythonpath); no [project] or [build-system]. `ls setup.py setup.cfg MANIFEST.in` -> all 'No such file or directory'; grep '\[project\]|\[build-system\]' across all *.toml returns nothing. server.json lines 6-15 advertise {registryType: pypi, identifier: 'universal-mail-automation', version: '0.1.0', transport: stdio}, generated verbatim by gen_server_json at scripts/gen_commerce_artifacts.py:82-89. So the tree cannot build/publish a PyPI dist of that name; an MCP registry client trusting the entry fails at install.
representative.rationale: Packaging metadata genuinely absent so the advertised PyPI artifact cannot be built from this tree; an mcp_server package WITH __main__.py exists (python -m mcp_server runs locally), so the gap is distribution-only and the repo is an unpublished 0.1.0 PUBLIC_PROCESS demo whose live surface is the Cloudflare worker, not PyPI — downgrade high->medium.

representative.location: whole file (no credit-debit/cap assertion); cf. tests/test_acp.py:117-153 and tests/test_store.py:35-43
representative.title: Test suite asserts credits are ADDED and that consume_credit works as a unit, but no test asserts a triage run ever DEBITS a credit or hits a cap — masking the unwired metering
representative.evidence: Triage path has zero metering: `grep` of api/service.py for consume_credit/entitlements_for/monthly_run_cap/run_credits/debit/cap returns NOTHING; api/app.py `_run` -> `service.run_triage` (service.py:70,109) carries no account/credit context and has no auth/Depends dependency on the triage endpoints. `consume_credit` exists only at api/store.py:235. Every test touching run_credits/consume_credit only ADDS or unit-tests in isolation: test_store.py:35-43 (`test_credits_atomic_no_overdraw` unit-tests consume_credit), test_store.py:84/88 (==100, add), test_acp.py:133 (==200 credited), test_acp.py:153/173 (==100, idempotency). grep across tests/ for run_credits/consume_credit/balance/debit/cap finds NO test that drives /v1/triage and asserts a balance DECREASE or a cap-block.
representative.rationale: Factual core fully verified: metering is unwired in the triage path and no debit-on-run/cap test exists; downgraded to medium because this is a contributory test-coverage gap (the masking), while the severity-bearing item is the unwired production metering itself.

representative.title: Smart mailbox ships with literal placeholder 'your-name' — 'Needs Action' view is broken/empty unless hand-edited
representative.evidence: Line 11 verbatim: `{condition type:sender, qualifier:does contain value:"your-name"}`. Comment at line 9 ('Replace "your-name" with your own name/email fragment') instructs replacement but nothing in the script enforces or prompts for it. Run as-is, the '1. Needs Action' mailbox filters senders containing the literal string 'your-name', matching nothing.
representative.rationale: Literal placeholder is real and the headline view is silently empty unless hand-edited; medium is fair for a UX-critical-but-manual run-once script with an inline replacement comment.

representative.title: Important-sender match uses substring `contains` on full From string — over-matches and is display-name spoofable
representative.evidence: Line 14 `set s to sender of msg` captures the full From string (display name + address); line 5 `if theSender contains s` is substring matching with no boundary anchoring. Contrast core/rules.py:659-663 `_domain_matches` whose docstring explicitly states it 'Kills substring embeds' and left-label spoofs — boundary logic this script does not share. The canonical VIP path (check_vip_sender, rules.py:873-887) uses re.search against validated patterns; this script bypasses it entirely.
representative.rationale: Both sub-claims hold against current code: unanchored substring match plus matching against the full From string (display-name-spoofable); diverges from the hardened Python gate. Medium is fair given flagging is non-destructive (worst case over-flagging).

representative.title: Domain match runs against full From string including display name — display-name spoofing routes legitimate mail
representative.evidence: Line 26 `set s to sender of msg`; line 9 tests `theSender contains ("@" & dom)` and `theSender ends with dom` against the whole string. providers/mailapp.py:150,190,193 reads `set msgSender to sender of msg` and assigns it directly to EmailMessage.sender with no address extraction, confirming `sender of msg` is the full From form ('Display Name <addr@domain>'). The Python gate instead parses via parseaddr (core/rules.py:746 docstring: 'RFC 5322-parses via parseaddr (the display NAME never feeds the gate)', line 755 `display, addr = parseaddr(decoded)`). The AppleScript does no such extraction, so `contains` can match display-name content (e.g. display name containing 'mailchimp.com').
representative.rationale: Confirmed: `sender of msg` carries display name + address, the `contains` test runs against the whole string, and the cited Python gate deliberately strips the display name — this script does not.

representative.title: Cloudflare smoke test validates only a thin happy-path slice and would not catch the method/route/contract divergences
representative.evidence: ci.yml:111-119 smoke test only asserts: /health contains '"status":"ok"', /app/ 2xx, /v1/billing/plans contains '"plans"', and a single PROTECTED sender (courts.ca.gov) returns '"protected":true'. Confirmed in worker.mjs that no /.well-known/agent.json, /server.json, or /openapi.json route exists (grep of pathname checks at lines 226-307 shows none), so those would 404 untested. Worker line 1 defines `const PROTECTED = new Set([...])` but senderCheck (lines 82+) uses `.includes("courts.ca.gov") || .endsWith(".gov")` and `.includes("chase.com")` — the PROTECTED set is indeed unused. No non-protected sender, wrong-method/405, /v1/triage vs preview, or /v1/audit/{run_id} is exercised.
representative.rationale: Coverage claims verify against the worker source; this is a real thin-slice smoke test. Downgraded to low: it is a post-deploy observability smoke test on a demo domain, not a unit/contract gate — limited blast radius.

representative.title: com.user.gmail_labeler.plist is orphaned — committed but never installed by deploy.sh (only booted out)
representative.evidence: deploy.sh:9-10 set PLIST_SRC/DEST to com.user.mail_automation.plist only; :48 copies only that; :57 references com.user.gmail_labeler ONLY to bootout. `git ls-files` shows com.user.gmail_labeler.plist is tracked and it exists on disk (970 bytes). No grep hit installs/copies it. The plist itself (line 6: <string>com.user.gmail_labeler</string>) invokes the same run_automation.sh as mail_automation.
representative.rationale: The gmail_labeler plist is committed and on-disk but has no installer path in deploy.sh (only a bootout), making it orphaned dead weight; hygiene/low is accurate.

representative.title: No error handling on list/batchModify; pagination relies on label removal and len<1000 heuristic, can loop
representative.evidence: No try/except anywhere in mark_read_loop; list().execute() (line 44-46) and batchModify().execute() (line 60) are unguarded. Any HttpError aborts with traceback. No backoff (contrast gmail_labeler.py _execute_with_backoff at line 78).
representative.rationale: Confirmed lack of error handling on Gmail API calls; severity appropriately low since it is read-state-only legacy.

representative.location: _init_labels lines 120-122; process_batch lines 187, 224-226; categorize via core returns 'Misc/Other'
representative.title: Dead 'Uncategorized' removal logic — core categorizer returns 'Misc/Other', not 'Uncategorized'
representative.evidence: grep confirms 'Uncategorized' appears NOWHERE in core/rules.py; the catch-all is 'Misc/Other' (rules.py:501 rule, :1038 `return best_match or "Misc/Other"`). gmail_labeler.py:131 delegates to _core_categorize (core.rules.categorize_message), so categorize_message() can never return 'Uncategorized'. Thus uncategorized_id (line 187) is None unless a literal 'Uncategorized' label pre-exists in the account (lines 121-122), and the guard `label_name != 'Uncategorized'` (line 225) is always-true dead trim. The 'Misc/Other' comments at 209-211 are stale relative to the variable name.
representative.rationale: Dead/stale removal branch confirmed against the real taxonomy; but it is benign no-op cleanup, not a data-loss bug, so severity drops from medium to low.

representative.title: httpx is a test-only dep shipped into the production image
representative.evidence: grep for 'import httpx|from httpx|httpx.' across api/, acp/, mcp_server/ and the whole tree returns ZERO hits in production .py files. Outbound HTTP in production uses `requests` (providers/outlook.py). httpx is consumed only by `fastapi.testclient.TestClient` (tests/test_api.py:3, test_billing.py:8, test_acp.py:5, test_receipts.py:3, test_web.py:3). Dockerfile L8-9 `COPY requirements.txt requirements-api.txt requirements-mcp.txt ./` + `RUN pip install ... -r requirements-api.txt` bakes httpx into the prod image. No requirements-dev.txt exists (only requirements.txt, requirements-api.txt, requirements-mcp.txt).
representative.rationale: Test-only dependency (httpx via TestClient) is installed wholesale into the production image; real hygiene smell, severity low is accurate.

representative.title: Destructive bulk label move has NO dry-run guard and runs immediately on execution
representative.evidence: grep for argparse/--apply/--dry-run/dry_run in auto_drain.py returns nothing. `__main__` (line 201-202) calls drain_loop() directly. batchModify executes immediately (line 178). icloud_triage.py by contrast defaults to dry-run and requires --apply (icloud_triage.py:12,86,154-156). Aggressive fallback 'Notification' confirmed at line 88.
representative.rationale: Accurate: no guard exists, in contrast to a sibling script. Downgraded to medium because the operation is label-to-label moves out of Misc/Other (not destructive deletion/INBOX removal), so harm is reversible mislabeling, not data loss.

representative.title: Dry-run inflates success_count and processed_count by the full action list, masking nothing applied
representative.evidence: Lines 283-300: in dry-run, `actions and not dry_run` is False so the else branch runs `result.success_count += len(actions)` (291); then unconditionally `processed_this_run += len(actions)` (293), `result.processed_count += len(actions)` (294), `total_processed += len(actions)` (295), and `if state: state.save(page_token, total_processed, ...)` (299-300). So dry-run reports success_count==processed_count and DOES mutate the state file.
representative.rationale: Two distinct real defects bundled: (a) dry-run counts previewed as 'successful' in print_stats, and (b) dry-run advances/persists state. (a) is cosmetic; (b) is the real harm. Severity medium not high — only manifests when --state-file is combined with --dry-run, a narrow combination, and the persisted page-token advance is the genuinely damaging part covered by U052.

representative.title: Dry-run persists/advances state file page token, corrupting later real resume
representative.evidence: Line 299-300 `if state: state.save(page_token, total_processed, stats, provider=provider.name)` runs inside the loop body with no `not dry_run` guard; also fires in the KeyboardInterrupt (318-319) and Exception (322-323) handlers. state.save (core/state.py:84-93) overwrites next_page_token and total_processed on disk. A later real run reads get_token() (line 172) and resumes from the advanced token, skipping previewed messages.
representative.rationale: Real: dry-run is not side-effect-free when --state-file is supplied; the persisted resume cursor advances so a subsequent real run skips the pages the dry-run only previewed. Same root cause as U051(b) but is a defensible standalone finding (state-file corruption path vs counter inflation). Downgraded high->medium: requires the specific --state-file + --dry-run combination, and --state-file defaults to none.

representative.title: Invalid user-supplied VIP regex pattern raises re.error and crashes categorization with no guard
representative.evidence: Reproduced: add_vip_sender('bad', pattern=r'[unclosed') then check_vip_sender('anyone@x.com') raises re.error (PatternError: 'unterminated character set'). Line 885 calls re.search(vip.pattern, ...) with no compile-time validation in add_vip_sender (lines 900-925) and no try/except.
representative.rationale: Real: an invalid user/config-supplied VIP regex crashes categorize_with_tier -> categorize_message for the whole run. Severity adjusted to medium: VIP_SENDERS ships EMPTY (lines 841-855 are all commented out), so this only triggers when a user adds a malformed pattern via config/add_vip_sender; not a default-path crash.

representative.title: AGENTS.md describes only the legacy Gmail scripts and omits the entire unified CLI / multi-provider / API architecture
representative.evidence: AGENTS.md lines 5-16 name gmail_labeler.py as 'core labeling logic', final_sweep.py, recount.py, and install via `pip install google-api-python-client google-auth-httplib2 google-auth-oauthlib`; line 26 states 'No unit test suite'. On disk the current architecture exists: cli.py (41KB), core/, providers/, api/ directories; tests/ contains conftest.py + test_acp.py/test_api.py/test_audit.py/test_billing.py/test_config.py/test_mailapp.py/test_mcp.py; pyproject.toml has [tool.pytest.ini_options]; .github/workflows/ci.yml references pytest. README.md/CLAUDE.md describe the unified multi-provider CLI. AGENTS.md presents the superseded legacy layout and falsely claims no tests.
representative.rationale: Every concrete claim verified against current disk: AGENTS.md is stale, points agents at wrong entry points, and falsely denies the existing pytest suite/CI gate; impact is documentation/onboarding only (no runtime defect), so medium holds.

representative.title: monthly_run_cap is consumed only by the marketing-artifact generator, never by runtime enforcement
representative.evidence: grep 'monthly_run_cap' --include='*.py': consumed only at scripts/gen_commerce_artifacts.py:46 (marketing render) and inside public_dict()/entitlements_for() in plans.py. No run-counting comparison exists. Confirmed no usage-accounting storage: store.py schema (41-98) has run_credits and current_period_end but no per-month counter; grep for runs_used/usage_count/monthly_runs/period_start/reset_at across the repo => NO USAGE TABLE FOUND.
representative.rationale: Monthly run cap is advertised in the public catalog but never enforced, and the data needed to enforce it is not recorded anywhere. Real money-path gap; medium is appropriate (advertised limit with no enforcement substrate).

representative.title: --limit counts built actions, not messages scanned; vip-only/protected/skip runs fetch far more than limit
representative.evidence: Loop guard `while processed_this_run < limit` (183) and `processed_this_run += len(actions)` (293). `actions` excludes protected senders (continue at 225), non-VIP in vip_only (continue at 230), and None details (continue at 207). batch_limit = min(limit - processed_this_run, 100) (185) keeps requesting full batches and page_token advances (298,313), so the loop scans far more than `limit` messages when many are filtered out. Docstring (line 156) says 'Max messages to process per run'.
representative.rationale: Confirmed off-by-semantics: with --vip-only over a large mailbox, processed_this_run only counts built actions, so the run can walk the entire mailbox (extra API calls + 1s sleeps per batch) before reaching limit. Diverges from documented behavior; runaway-cost risk is real but bounded by mailbox size, so medium.

representative.title: cmd_escalate trusts age_hours=0 without detecting that the provider supplied no date
representative.evidence: age_hours = calculate_email_age_hours(msg.date) (883); escalate_by_age(email_age_hours=age_hours,...) (886). calculate_email_age_hours returns 0 when date is None (rules.py:1163-1166). Verified gmail (providers/gmail.py:204-209, 286-291), imap (261-266), mailapp (240-245) construct EmailMessage with NO date= kwarg (defaults None, core/models.py:48). Only outlook sets date (outlook.py:464,515). So for gmail/imap/mailapp age_hours is always 0, escalate_by_age never escalates, yet summary prints 'Messages escalated: 0' (937) with no warning.
representative.rationale: Confirmed: for 3 of 4 providers escalate is a guaranteed no-op presented as a successful triage. The command gives false assurance with no 'date unsupported' warning. Medium — escalate is a documented user-facing command and the silent zero is misleading.

representative.location: senderCheck lines 82-170 vs core/rules.py is_protected_sender (787-805), _decode_mime (624-633), _idna_decode (636-649), relay handling (676+)
representative.title: Worker omits MIME-decode, IDNA/punycode normalization, iCloud-relay recovery and self-mailbox match — homoglyph/relay/encoded protected senders are unprotected on the live site
representative.evidence: senderCheck (82-170) does String(sender).trim().toLowerCase() then substring/endsWith checks — no MIME decode, no IDNA/punycode, no iCloud relay recovery, no self match. core/rules.py confirms _decode_mime (RFC2047), _idna_decode (xn-- per-label), _relay_domain_candidates/_resolve_addr (iCloud relay), _self_match (Gmail canonical) all feed is_protected_sender.
representative.rationale: Confirmed: the worker omits every normalization layer the engine applies, so encoded/homoglyph/relayed protected senders evade protection on the demo. Medium — over/under-protection on a non-canonical demo surface. Echoing corroborated:false.

representative.title: Tech/Security greedy .* patterns elevate ordinary marketing mail to Tier 1 Critical
representative.evidence: Reproduced: categorize_with_tier('shop@gadgets.com','new mobile device unboxing review') -> label=Tech/Security, tier=1. r'new.*device' (line 216) is unanchored greedy and matches the marketing subject; Tech/Security is tier 1 (line 224), priority 9, which beats Shopping(10)/Marketing(17).
representative.rationale: Real: greedy unanchored patterns elevate gadget marketing to Critical/starred. Medium stands (false-positive inflation of the Critical bucket; no data loss since tier-1 is kept-in-inbox).

representative.location: Personal/Government patterns lines 472-478 (r'passport', r'dmv', r'social.*security', r'state\.fl\.us')
representative.title: Government Tier-1 patterns 'passport'/'dmv'/'social.*security' bare-match generic subjects
representative.evidence: Reproduced: categorize_with_tier('x@y.io','a passport to better savings') -> Personal/Government tier 1; ('x@y.io','social security number protection guide') -> Personal/Government tier 1. Bare patterns r'passport' (473), r'social.*security' (474) match generic marketing subjects; tier 1 at line 481.
representative.rationale: Real: bare Government keywords elevate generic copy to Critical/starred. Medium (false-positive inflation, no archival loss since tier-1 kept in inbox).

representative.location: whole file — no POST /acp/checkout_sessions/{id} update test (grep confirms only create/get/complete/cancel exercised)
representative.title: update_session (a price/items-mutating, money-relevant endpoint) has zero test coverage
representative.evidence: grep of tests/ for 'checkout_sessions/' shows only GET (lines 98, 100), /complete, and /cancel against {id}; no bare POST /acp/checkout_sessions/{sid}. router.py:200-232 update_session re-derives line_items/total_runs (217-225), guards invalid state (211-214), and re-prices on items=None via a two-source read (223-224) — none of this is exercised by any test.
representative.rationale: Grep confirms the money-mutating update endpoint has zero regression coverage; claim holds exactly as stated.

representative.title: Resolving Archive mailbox can throw before the loop with no handling
representative.evidence: Lines 9-13 resolve `set targetMailbox to mailbox targetMailboxName` (default "Archive", L4) / `mailbox ... of account ...` with no surrounding try. If no top-level mailbox literally named "Archive" exists (default targetAccountName is "", L5), this raises an unhandled AppleScript error before the repeat loop, aborting the run.
representative.rationale: Statically the resolve is outside any error handler and the default depends on a not-guaranteed mailbox name; real but config-conditional, so medium with the understanding it only fires when 'Archive' is absent.

representative.title: Page-token resumption with a draining/destructive query can skip messages or resume against a shifted result set
representative.evidence: Lines 285-286 load a saved page token only for query=='has:nouserlabels'; lines 321-322 persist nextPageToken for that same default query. Labeling unlabeled mail removes it from the 'has:nouserlabels' match-set, so a resumed nextPageToken indexes into a shifted/shrunken result set. The author's own comment (272-282) calls page tokens 'dangerous' for draining queries, yet the default query IS a draining query and the code still persists+reuses its token. Gmail can skip messages between the consumed pages and the saved token's offset.
representative.rationale: Real silent-incomplete-coverage hazard on resume for the default draining query, exactly contradicting the in-file warning; medium is fair.

representative.title: tier_routing target_folder is never honored by Outlook move path — tier folder routing is effectively a no-op for the actual move
representative.evidence: Verified base.apply_actions (342-345): it iterates ONLY action.add_labels and calls apply_label(message_id, label); it never reads action.target_folder. cli.py:257 sets action.target_folder = tier_config.folder but add_labels still holds the categorization label (cli.py:243). For Outlook, apply_label moves to ensure_label_exists(label)=the category folder, NOT the tier Action/* folder.
representative.rationale: Confirmed cross-file gap: target_folder is set but never consumed by any provider's apply path, so Outlook tier 'Action folders' moves are a no-op (only the category color is applied). Medium — documented feature partially non-functional. corroborated=false echoed.

representative.title: Default bulkDomains ships an example domain 'newsletter.yourfav.com' and runs unedited
representative.evidence: Line 2: `set bulkDomains to {"mailchimp.com", "substack.com", "newsletter.yourfav.com"}`. First two are live real domains; third is a placeholder. The file contains no dry-run guard or confirmation prompt (no occurrence of dry-run/dryRun in the 33 lines), and line 28 unconditionally `move msg to bulkMailbox`. Run unedited, it immediately moves all mailchimp.com and substack.com inbox mail out to Newsletters.
representative.rationale: Confirmed: shipped defaults contain live domains and the script applies destructive moves with no dry-run, so running unedited acts on real mail.

representative.title: Payment client is cached at module level keyed on first-call env; a STRIPE_SECRET_KEY set after first call (or rotated) is never picked up
representative.evidence: payment.py:106-110 memoizes _CLIENT on first call from os.environ at that instant. If the process resolves before STRIPE_SECRET_KEY is set, NullPaymentClient is cached and every /complete returns 402 'payment is not configured' until set_payment_client(None) or restart. Contrast billing.py:68 (_client reads key per call). set_payment_client exists (113-117) but is test-only.
representative.rationale: Confirmed caching/late-key divergence. Severity low: deploy-ordering edge case, fail-closed direction (no free credits), recoverable by restart.

representative.location: apply_actions lines 356-363 (star/category for protected senders on folder providers)
