findings.title: CI smoke test only asserts /health, /app/, plans-present, and one courts.ca.gov sender — none of the worker's protection/receipt divergences are caught
findings.evidence: ci.yml smoke test (verified lines ~108-122): curls /health for status ok, /app/, /v1/billing/plans for 'plans', and POST /v1/senders/check {sender:'clerk@courts.ca.gov'} expecting protected:true. It does not test apple.com/irs.gov/docusign, no spoofed substring, no /v1/audit fetch, no triage-vs-preview comparison. courts.ca.gov passes because includes('courts.ca.gov') is true.
findings.rationale: Confirmed coverage gap, but this is test-quality (a missing-assertion observation), not a code defect — low severity. Echoing corroborated:false.

findings.location: senderCheck lines 82-170 vs core/rules.py is_protected_sender (787-805), _decode_mime (624-633), _idna_decode (636-649), relay handling (676+)
findings.title: Worker omits MIME-decode, IDNA/punycode normalization, iCloud-relay recovery and self-mailbox match — homoglyph/relay/encoded protected senders are unprotected on the live site
findings.evidence: senderCheck (82-170) does String(sender).trim().toLowerCase() then substring/endsWith checks — no MIME decode, no IDNA/punycode, no iCloud relay recovery, no self match. core/rules.py confirms _decode_mime (RFC2047), _idna_decode (xn-- per-label), _relay_domain_candidates/_resolve_addr (iCloud relay), _self_match (Gmail canonical) all feed is_protected_sender.
findings.rationale: Confirmed: the worker omits every normalization layer the engine applies, so encoded/homoglyph/relayed protected senders evade protection on the demo. Medium — over/under-protection on a non-canonical demo surface. Echoing corroborated:false.

findings.title: Agent-discovery surfaces /.well-known/agent.json, /server.json, /pricing.md 404 on the Cloudflare share demo
findings.evidence: Worker hardcodes only /llms.txt (307-318); all other non-API paths -> serveApp -> env.ASSETS.fetch. wrangler.toml: [assets] directory='./web', binding='ASSETS'. web/ contains ONLY index.html (ls confirms). Repo-root .well-known/agent.json, server.json, pricing.md, llms.txt exist but are NOT under web/, so /.well-known/agent.json, /server.json, /pricing.md 404 on the worker. DEPLOY.md:65 advertises GET /.well-known/agent.json + /llms.txt.
findings.rationale: Core claim confirmed (those discovery paths 404 on the worker; only the inlined /llms.txt responds and can drift from root llms.txt). One inaccuracy: the worker DOES serve /llms.txt (so 'even llms.txt' 404ing is wrong — it is served, just inlined). Largely overlaps U058. Medium.

findings.title: Worker senderCheck protected-sender logic is partially dead/incorrect vs documented protection set
findings.evidence: PROTECTED (line 1) never referenced (dead constant) — confirmed by grep of the file. Inline logic: .gov broad (106), chase (128), 1password fallthrough (153, with dead chase disjunct). README/llms.txt claim gov/financial/legal/platform protection. Claim 'core/rules.py PROTECTED_SENDERS has ~40 domains (stripe.com, robinhood.com, ...)' is INACCURATE: EXAMPLE_PROTECTED_SENDERS has ~17 entries and does NOT contain stripe.com or robinhood.com (those names do not appear in the protected list).
findings.rationale: Dead-set + narrow-coverage defect is real and confirmed (overlaps U451/U057/U053), but the specific count '~40 domains' and the named examples stripe.com/robinhood.com are fabricated, so partial. Low severity as a documentation-drift/dead-code item.

findings.title: Wrong-method requests to API routes fall through to the static asset server instead of returning 405
findings.evidence: Every /v1/* branch is guarded by '&& request.method === "POST"' (250,255,260,269,273,277) or '=== "GET"' (265,281). A wrong-method request (e.g. GET /v1/senders/check) does not match and falls through to return serveApp(request, env) at line 320 -> env.ASSETS.fetch -> SPA HTML/404, never a 405 with Allow. FastAPI returns 405+Allow for these.
findings.rationale: Confirmed routing behavior. Low severity — a demo share surface returning HTML for wrong-method probes is cosmetic/contract-hygiene, not a functional or safety defect. Echoing corroborated:false.

findings.title: /v1/audit/ with an empty run_id fabricates a receipt for the literal id 'demo'
findings.evidence: Line 282: const runId = url.pathname.split('/').pop() || 'demo'. For '/v1/audit/' (trailing slash) .pop() returns '' -> runId='demo' -> 200 receipt 'Signed receipt for demo'. For '/v1/audit/a/b/c', .pop() returns 'c' -> receipt for 'c', ignoring the rest. No 400/404. api/receipts.py treats {run_id} as one param and 404s on miss.
findings.rationale: Confirmed path-parse behavior. Low — it is a sub-aspect of the already-confirmed fabricated/unsigned audit surface (U055/U171); the path-robustness angle adds little independent risk on a demo.

findings.title: Worker /v1/billing/plans omits metered/credit_packs/currency that the real API returns
findings.evidence: worker billingPlans (200-214) returns only {plans:[...]}. api/billing.py list_plans (verified) returns {plans: plans.public_catalog(), metered: plans.METERED_ADDON, credit_packs: list(plans.CREDIT_PACKS.values()), currency: 'usd'}. So data.metered/credit_packs/currency are undefined against the worker.
findings.rationale: Confirmed contract divergence. Low — the demo's plans payload is a subset; a client expecting credit_packs/currency gets undefined, but the worker is explicitly a non-canonical review surface.

findings.title: readJson swallows all parse errors to {} so malformed/empty/non-JSON POST bodies return a 200 'success' instead of 400
findings.evidence: readJson (216-222) catch {} returns {} on any parse failure. So POST /v1/senders/check with malformed/empty body -> body={} -> senderCheck(undefined) -> empty-value branch (84-104) returns protected:true; triage endpoints -> triagePreview('demo', NaN) -> 200 fabricated receipt. FastAPI returns 422 on schema violation.
findings.rationale: Confirmed: bad input never yields 4xx, always a plausible 200. Low — fail-closed protected:true on empty is actually the engine's documented behavior (is_protected_sender returns True on empty); the only real gap is the missing 422 on malformed bodies on a demo surface.

findings.title: Preview always returns run_id 'demo_preview', but the UI/llms.txt promise 'each run gets a run_id' fetchable at /v1/audit/{run_id}
findings.evidence: triagePreview hardcodes run_id:'demo_preview' (line 196). index.html:642 'Each run gets a run_id. Fetch the receipt at GET /v1/audit/{run_id}'. Fetching /v1/audit/demo_preview returns hardcoded total:3/protected_held:2/archived:1 (288-304), which need not match the preview's own total=max(capped,3)/protectedHeld math (175,181). So the 'verifiable receipt' counts can differ from the preview they claim to certify.
findings.rationale: Confirmed state inconsistency between preview output and audit lookup. Low — both are fabricated demo values; the inconsistency is a cosmetic trust-surface issue overlapping U171/U172.

findings.title: Two plists invoke the same run_automation.sh at the same 9:00 time — duplicate/overlapping schedule risk if both loaded
findings.evidence: diff com.user.gmail_labeler.plist com.user.mail_automation.plist shows ONLY two deltas: line6 Label (com.user.gmail_labeler vs com.user.mail_automation) and line26 RunAtLoad (<true/> vs <false/>). Both share ProgramArguments=/bin/bash run_automation.sh, StartCalendarInterval Hour=9 Minute=0, and identical log paths /Users/4jp/System/Logs/mail_automation/launchd.std{out,err}.log. The 'double-processing if both loaded' claim is factually accurate for the byte-identical schedule/logs.
findings.rationale: The structural duplication and identical 9:00 schedule + shared log paths are confirmed, but deploy.sh actively boots OUT com.user.gmail_labeler (line 57) and only bootstraps mail_automation (line 60), so the standard install path never loads both; the concurrent-double-run is only reachable via manual mis-load, so severity is low not medium.

findings.title: Non-standard log directory (~/System/Logs) hardcoded — launchd will not create it and will fail to start the job if missing
findings.evidence: StandardOutPath/StandardErrorPath = /Users/4jp/System/Logs/mail_automation/ (lines 15,17) — non-standard vs ~/Library/Logs, confirmed. Same paths in com.user.gmail_labeler.plist:15,17 (grep confirmed). deploy.sh:11 sets LOG_DIR="$HOME/System/Logs/mail_automation" and deploy.sh:38 `mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR"` — so on the supported deploy path the directory IS created (for the deploying user's $HOME). I confirmed ~/System/Logs/mail_automation does NOT currently exist on disk, so absent a deploy run launchd's redirect would fail. The two only line up for user 4jp, exactly as the finding states.
findings.rationale: Mechanism is accurate but the impact is overstated: deploy.sh (the documented installer) creates the dir before bootstrapping, so the supported path works for user 4jp; the failure is contingent on a different-$HOME user or running the plist without deploy.sh. Effectively a sub-case of the U061 hardcoded-home root cause; kept as partial/low rather than duplicate since it adds the launchd-wont-create-parent-dir detail.

findings.location: lines 197-210 (record/_append entry), redact gating at 209-210; docstring 40-42
findings.title: Redacted ('shareable/committable') audit JSONL still writes raw provider message_id on every line
findings.evidence: audit.py line 200 always sets entry["message_id"]=message_id inside the entry dict; the ONLY redact-gated field is sender (lines 209-210: `if not self.redact: entry["sender"]=sender`). Docstring 40-42 says redact=True yields 'domain-only lines ... a receipt you can share or commit'; cli.py:380 advertises --redact-audit as 'a shareable domain-only receipt'. So a redacted JSONL still embeds the raw provider message_id on every line.
findings.rationale: Real: redaction promise covers sender but not message_id; for IMAP UIDs / Outlook / Mail.app store paths these can be internal identifiers, contradicting the 'committable' claim. Severity downgraded to low because message_ids are opaque per-mailbox tokens (not PII like the local-part the redaction explicitly targets) and the dominant share-blocker (sender address) IS redacted.

findings.title: BATCH_SIZE env var crashes on non-integer value
findings.evidence: Same defect as U062: unguarded `int(os.getenv(f'{prefix}BATCH_SIZE'))` at line 273 raising ValueError out of load_config().
findings.rationale: Identical root cause to U062 (BATCH_SIZE bare int cast); de-duplicated.

findings.title: YAML batch_size/port/throttle_seconds assigned verbatim with no type validation or coercion
findings.evidence: Lines 194-195 (batch_size), 196-197 (throttle_seconds), 217-218 (imap.port) copy raw yaml.safe_load values into typed fields with no coercion. Verified: no isinstance/int/float guard on these scalar provider/general fields. However the 'downstream TypeError' claim is weakened for batch_size/throttle/default_provider — batch_size and throttle_seconds are never read outside tests (grep shows only tests/test_config.py consumes them), so the 'mis-sizes batches / time.sleep TypeError' scenario does not occur in current code paths. imap.port flowing into imaplib is plausible but unverified in providers.
findings.rationale: Real lack of validation, but the asserted downstream blast radius is overstated — the dead fields can't blow up downstream; downgrade to low.

findings.title: default_provider taken verbatim from YAML/env with no validation against the known provider set
findings.evidence: default_provider assigned unvalidated from YAML (189) and env (266-267). Confirmed dead in CLI: grep shows config.default_provider is read only in tests/test_config.py; cli.py uses args.provider exclusively (cli.py:426,463,495,527,625,731,832). The config object returned by load_config() is consumed only by apply_vip_senders_from_config.
findings.rationale: Both halves confirmed (no validation AND the knob is dead — provider comes from --provider, not config), but it's a silently-ignored config field, not a crash; low severity is appropriate.

findings.title: log_level loaded but never applied, and not validated against valid logging levels
findings.evidence: config.log_level populated at config.py:191 (YAML) and 268-269 (env). cli.py:48-51 hardcodes `logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO)`; only override is cli.py:1194-1195 `if args.verbose: logging.getLogger().setLevel(logging.DEBUG)`. grep confirms config.log_level is read only in tests. So `log_level: ERROR` in config.yaml has zero effect; value is also unvalidated.
findings.rationale: Confirmed dead config knob plus missing validation, matching the slice claim; low severity (cosmetic/UX surprise, no crash since the value is never applied).

findings.title: custom_rules loaded into Config but never applied to the rules engine (dead config knob)
findings.evidence: grep -rn custom_rules: only config.py:96 (field), 249-250 (assignment), 389 (sample comment). No apply_custom_rules anywhere; cli.py and core/rules.py never read config.custom_rules. There is no equivalent to apply_vip_senders_from_config for custom_rules. Documented in CLAUDE.md/sample as 'override default LABEL_RULES'.
findings.rationale: Confirmed dead config knob — custom_rules is loaded and silently never merged into LABEL_RULES; low severity (silently-ignored feature, not a crash).

findings.title: calculate_email_age_hours returns 0 for None date, conflating 'unknown age' with 'brand new' and masking the provider gap
findings.evidence: Lines 1173-1174 return 0 when email_date is None. Confirmed gmail.py/imap.py/mailapp.py never set date= on EmailMessage (grep: only outlook.py sets date= at lines 464,515; models.py:48 defaults date=None). cli.py:667/883 call calculate_email_age_hours(msg.date); escalate_by_age line 1122 takes the <24h no-escalation branch for age 0.
findings.rationale: Real root-cause enabler: age-0 default silently disables age-based escalation on 3 of 4 providers with no signal. Medium is appropriate (feature degradation, not a crash; escalation is an opt-in triage feature).

findings.title: Tech/Security greedy .* patterns elevate ordinary marketing mail to Tier 1 Critical
findings.evidence: Reproduced: categorize_with_tier('shop@gadgets.com','new mobile device unboxing review') -> label=Tech/Security, tier=1. r'new.*device' (line 216) is unanchored greedy and matches the marketing subject; Tech/Security is tier 1 (line 224), priority 9, which beats Shopping(10)/Marketing(17).
findings.rationale: Real: greedy unanchored patterns elevate gadget marketing to Critical/starred. Medium stands (false-positive inflation of the Critical bucket; no data loss since tier-1 is kept-in-inbox).

findings.location: Personal/Government patterns lines 472-478 (r'passport', r'dmv', r'social.*security', r'state\.fl\.us')
findings.title: Government Tier-1 patterns 'passport'/'dmv'/'social.*security' bare-match generic subjects
findings.evidence: Reproduced: categorize_with_tier('x@y.io','a passport to better savings') -> Personal/Government tier 1; ('x@y.io','social security number protection guide') -> Personal/Government tier 1. Bare patterns r'passport' (473), r'social.*security' (474) match generic marketing subjects; tier 1 at line 481.
findings.rationale: Real: bare Government keywords elevate generic copy to Critical/starred. Medium (false-positive inflation, no archival loss since tier-1 kept in inbox).

findings.title: State save failure is logged and swallowed; corrupt state file falls back to default silently, risking reprocessing or skipped resumption
findings.evidence: Combines the same swallowed-save-exception (U073) and non-atomic-write-resets-to-default (U070) defects; clear()-swallow overlaps U589. Same root causes already adjudicated.
findings.rationale: No new root cause beyond U073/U070; bundled restatement.

findings.title: State file written non-atomically; crash mid-write corrupts resume state
findings.evidence: Identical claim to U070: open('w') truncating + json.dump, crash leaves partial file, _load resets to default. (category 'resource-leak' is mislabeled — it is a non-atomic-write issue.)
findings.rationale: Same non-atomic-write root cause as U070.

findings.title: last_run uses naive local time while the rest of the codebase is UTC-aware
findings.evidence: core/state.py:87 `datetime.now().isoformat()` (naive local) vs core/audit.py:198 `datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()` and core/rules.py:1179 `datetime.now(timezone.utc)`. Inconsistency is real.
findings.rationale: Confirmed naive-vs-UTC inconsistency, but no code currently parses/compares last_run (grep shows only getter + tests), so the TypeError scenarios are latent. Downgraded from medium to low.

findings.title: Non-atomic write: a crash or kill during save() can leave a truncated/corrupt state file
findings.evidence: Restates U070/U194 — open('w') truncation + crash leaves empty/partial file, _load defeats crash recovery. Same line 92.
findings.rationale: Same non-atomic-write root cause.

findings.title: Smart mailbox ships with literal placeholder 'your-name' — 'Needs Action' view is broken/empty unless hand-edited
findings.evidence: Line 11 verbatim: `{condition type:sender, qualifier:does contain value:"your-name"}`. Comment at line 9 ('Replace "your-name" with your own name/email fragment') instructs replacement but nothing in the script enforces or prompts for it. Run as-is, the '1. Needs Action' mailbox filters senders containing the literal string 'your-name', matching nothing.
findings.rationale: Literal placeholder is real and the headline view is silently empty unless hand-edited; medium is fair for a UX-critical-but-manual run-once script with an inline replacement comment.

findings.title: CTA "View on GitHub" link points to stale/renamed org (organvm-iii-ergon) that only resolves via GitHub's rename redirect
findings.evidence: Line 242 verbatim: View on GitHub. Real git remote is https://github.com/a-organvm/universal-mail--automation.git; server.json line 3 declares io.github.a-organvm/universal-mail-automation. README badges still use organvm-iii-ergon too (lines 1,7,252,...). So the canonical org is a-organvm and the CTA points at the renamed/stale org.
findings.rationale: The wrong-canonical-URL defect is real and confirmed, but the page is an undeployed orphan (see U466), so the dependence-on-redirect / takeover blast radius is much smaller than medium; downgraded to low.

findings.title: Pitch page shows placeholder marketing copy that contradicts the shipped, detailed pricing and feature set
findings.evidence: Line 177: feature card 'In Development / Feature list coming soon.'; lines 200-204: 'Business model details coming soon.' with badges 'subscription'/'pre-launch'; hero/solution/cta repeat the meta description 'Email workflow automation and scheduling' (lines 142,166,240). Contradicts shipped pricing: api/plans.py has Free / Pro $19/mo (price_cents=1900) / Business $49/mo (price_cents=4900), $0.01/triage metered, ACP credit packs; pricing.md (1379 bytes) exists. README ships full product description.
findings.rationale: Placeholder copy genuinely contradicts the shipped pricing/feature surfaces; defect confirmed, but severity is low because the file is an undeployed orphan not seen by any prospect.

findings.title: Important-sender match uses substring `contains` on full From string — over-matches and is display-name spoofable
findings.evidence: Line 14 `set s to sender of msg` captures the full From string (display name + address); line 5 `if theSender contains s` is substring matching with no boundary anchoring. Contrast core/rules.py:659-663 `_domain_matches` whose docstring explicitly states it 'Kills substring embeds' and left-label spoofs — boundary logic this script does not share. The canonical VIP path (check_vip_sender, rules.py:873-887) uses re.search against validated patterns; this script bypasses it entirely.
findings.rationale: Both sub-claims hold against current code: unanchored substring match plus matching against the full From string (display-name-spoofable); diverges from the hardened Python gate. Medium is fair given flagging is non-destructive (worst case over-flagging).

findings.title: Refresh catches only RefreshError; transient network errors crash and a post-refresh write-back failure is not isolated
findings.evidence: Lines 166-173: `try: creds.refresh(Request()); store_token_info(creds) except RefreshError: creds = None`. The single try wraps BOTH the refresh and the write-back, and the only handler is `except RefreshError` (imported line 11). google.auth.exceptions.TransportError (transient network failure during refresh) is a sibling of RefreshError, not a subclass, so it propagates uncaught. store_token_info (line 168) can raise RuntimeError ('Token storage not configured' line 147, or '1Password CLI failed/not found' from _run_op lines 30/33/35) AFTER a successful refresh — also not RefreshError — crashing the call and discarding the freshly-refreshed token.
findings.rationale: Both claimed escape paths are real: TransportError is not a RefreshError, and the write-back is not isolated from the refresh try; a post-refresh storage failure loses the refreshed token. Medium is fair.

findings.location: _init_labels lines 120-122; process_batch lines 187, 224-226; categorize via core returns 'Misc/Other'
findings.title: Dead 'Uncategorized' removal logic — core categorizer returns 'Misc/Other', not 'Uncategorized'
findings.evidence: grep confirms 'Uncategorized' appears NOWHERE in core/rules.py; the catch-all is 'Misc/Other' (rules.py:501 rule, :1038 `return best_match or "Misc/Other"`). gmail_labeler.py:131 delegates to _core_categorize (core.rules.categorize_message), so categorize_message() can never return 'Uncategorized'. Thus uncategorized_id (line 187) is None unless a literal 'Uncategorized' label pre-exists in the account (lines 121-122), and the guard `label_name != 'Uncategorized'` (line 225) is always-true dead trim. The 'Misc/Other' comments at 209-211 are stale relative to the variable name.
findings.rationale: Dead/stale removal branch confirmed against the real taxonomy; but it is benign no-op cleanup, not a data-loss bug, so severity drops from medium to low.

findings.location: LABEL_RULES line 282; categorize_email line 364; label_all_unlabeled_emails lines 386,430
findings.title: Legacy catch-all label is 'Uncategorized' while core uses 'Misc/Other' (taxonomy divergence)
findings.evidence: Legacy line 282 defines catch-all 'Uncategorized' (priority 999); line 364 returns `best_match or "Uncategorized"`; lines 383-386 create+cache it and lines 430-432 apply it. core/rules.py line 501/1038 uses 'Misc/Other'. run_automation.sh:37 sweeps `--query "label:Misc/Other"` and bulk_sweeper.py SWEEP_RULES all query `label:Misc/Other`, so 'Uncategorized'-labeled mail is never re-swept. Split-brain confirmed.
findings.rationale: Real taxonomy divergence, but downgraded from medium to low: the legacy file is not wired into any automation/CLI; only a user manually running this specific file would create the orphaned 'Uncategorized' label.

findings.location: LABEL_RULES (whole dict, lines 39-283) vs core/rules.py LABEL_RULES
findings.title: Legacy LABEL_RULES is a stale fork missing many categories and hardened patterns
findings.evidence: core/rules.py uses anchored patterns: r"(^|@|\.)gemini\.com\b" (line 147, comment 'crypto exchange, not Google Gemini AI'), r"(^|@|\.)citi(bank|cards|group)?\.com\b" (155, 'not publicity/felicity'), r"(^|@|\.)ally\.com\b" (157, 'not really/Allyson'), and de-ranks AI/Services to priority 12 (line 125). Legacy uses bare r"gemini"/r"citi"/r"ally" and AI/Services priority 4. AST test confirmed legacy over-matches: 'really good newsletter' -> Finance/Banking (bare 'ally'), 'noreply@gemini.com google AI' -> Finance/Banking (bare 'gemini'). NOTE: 'publicity'/'felicity' did NOT over-match to Finance in my test (they fell to Uncategorized) because bare 'citi' requires the substring 'citi' which neither contains.
findings.rationale: The over-matching and stale-fork claims are real and verified for gemini/ally; the publicity/felicity example in the finding text is inaccurate (those don't contain 'citi'). Downgraded to low because the legacy file is unwired and relabel-only.

findings.location: LABEL_RULES lines 60, 95, 235, 239, 273 (patterns r"render\.com", r"self\.inc", r"builtin\.com", r"e\.godaddy\.com", r"ibo\.org")
findings.title: Double-backslash regex patterns match a literal backslash, so these senders never categorize
findings.evidence: Same double-backslash root cause and same line set as U075 (U205 is a subset: render/self/builtin/e.godaddy/ibo; U075 adds pieces/meta). Render is line 62 not 60. core/rules.py fixed these to single-backslash (lines 96 render\.com, 151 self\.inc, 378 builtin\.com, 405 e\.godaddy\.com).
findings.rationale: Same defect as U075 in the same file; merged. Render line cited as 60 (actual 62).

findings.location: main, slice line 144: uids = uids[max(0, len(uids) - args.start - args.limit) : len(uids) - args.start]
findings.title: Paging slice computes a negative end index when start > len(uids), selecting wrong (oldest) messages
findings.evidence: Reproduced exactly: python3 with uids=range(10), start=15, limit=5 -> slice end index = len(uids)-start = -5 (negative), so the slice [max(0,-10):-5] = uids[0:-5] = [0,1,2,3,4] (the 5 OLDEST messages) instead of []. Line 144 uses an unclamped end index.
findings.rationale: Real off-by-paging bug: paging past the end silently selects oldest messages instead of an empty page; end should be max(0, len(uids)-args.start). Blast radius limited (only when start>len and DRY_RUN unset; default DRY_RUN=1), so medium is fair.

findings.title: All advertised endpoints use placeholder host mail.example.com, not the live uma.4444j99.dev
findings.evidence: llms.txt lines 6-14 do hardcode https://mail.example.com. But this static file is a GENERATED artifact: scripts/gen_commerce_artifacts.py:28 sets BASE = os.environ.get('PUBLIC_BASE_URL', 'https://mail.example.com') and writes it via build_llms_txt(BASE) (lines 99-100). DEPLOY.md:79 documents PUBLIC_BASE_URL=https://<host> as the override. Critically, the live server serves /llms.txt DYNAMICALLY with the real request host: api/well_known.py:84-85 returns build_llms_txt(str(request.base_url)). So an agent doing GET /llms.txt against the deployed host never sees mail.example.com. mail.example.com is also an RFC-2606 reserved placeholder, not a typo'd real host.
findings.rationale: Real (committed file shows the placeholder) but overstated: it is an intentional generator default with a documented override and the runtime endpoint resolves the live host, so 'every integration fails' does not hold.

findings.title: Headline trust claim ('signed audit receipt', 'never archives government/financial/legal/platform mail') contradicts the live worker.mjs
findings.evidence: The finding compares this llms.txt against cloudflare/worker.mjs, but this static llms.txt is generated from api/well_known.py (build_llms_txt) and served by the FastAPI app — NOT the worker. On the FastAPI surface the headline claims are accurate: api/receipts.py implements a real HMAC-SHA256 signed, re-derivable receipt (sign/verify at lines 66-74, SIGNATURE_ALGORITHM='HMAC-SHA256'), and the gate is genuinely fail-closed — api/app.py:112-122 raises HTTP 500 'SAFETY GATE VIOLATION' on service.AuditInvariantError, never 200. worker.mjs is a separate 'share demo' that serves its OWN shorter /llms.txt (worker.mjs:307-318), so its hardcoded receipt does not describe this file's advertised surface.
findings.rationale: Mis-attributes the demo worker's behavior to the FastAPI surface that actually emits/serves this llms.txt, where the signed-receipt and fail-closed-gate claims are true.

findings.title: mail_export.tsv is git-tracked; currently header-only but the schema is sender/subject PII and the file is the AppleScript export target
findings.evidence: git ls-files --error-unmatch mail_export.tsv succeeds (tracked). git check-ignore exits 1 (NOT gitignored), while .gitignore DOES list config/protected_senders.local.txt and audit/*.jsonl for the same PII reason. Read shows exactly one content line: the header `mailbox_name\tmessage_id\tsender\tsubject\tdate_received\tread\tflagged\tsize_bytes` with zero data rows (file is 77 bytes). HOWEVER export_mail_snapshot.applescript line 1 writes to `(POSIX path of (path to home folder)) & "mail_automation/mail_export.tsv"` = /Users/4jp/mail_automation/mail_export.tsv, which is NOT the repo-root tracked file /Users/4jp/Code/organvm/universal-mail--automation/mail_export.tsv.
findings.rationale: Real hygiene issue (tracked PII-schema file, no .gitignore entry, against the repo's own precedent), but overstated: no PII leaks today (header-only) AND the export script does not write to this repo path, so it is not the 'exact output target' claimed — leak would require a manual copy into the repo, not a single run; severity downgraded medium->low.

findings.title: Docstring says '>30 days' but query uses 'older_than:7d' (also comment says 7 days) — stale/inconsistent threshold
findings.evidence: Line 3 docstring: 'Mark old (>30 days) emails ... as READ'. Line 41: query = f"label:{category} is:unread older_than:7d". Lines 39-40 inline comment also says '7 days'. The docstring's 30-day claim contradicts the actual 7-day threshold.
findings.rationale: Real doc/behavior mismatch on a mutating op, but blast radius is read-state only (removes UNREAD, never archives/deletes) so medium is inflated; downgraded to low.

findings.title: MCP triage tool does not bound/validate query and remove_label inputs
findings.evidence: MCP triage/triage_preview accept raw str `query` and Optional[str] `remove_label` (lines 100,112,114) and pass them straight to service.run_triage (132-135); only `limit` is clamped (_clamp_limit, 83-84). schemas.py bounds the HTTP path: query max_length=2048 (line 46), remove_label max_length=512 (line 49). So the MCP surface bypasses those caps.
findings.rationale: Real divergence — MCP query/remove_label are unbounded vs HTTP. But the engine's hard cap is `limit` (clamped to MAX_TRIAGE_LIMIT=1000), which bounds the actual provider work; an oversized query string is comparatively cheap, so the DoS framing is mild — low not medium.

findings.location: _triage() lines 129-146 and triage()/triage_preview() return paths; cf. api/app.py:_run lines 124-130
findings.title: MCP triage returns no persisted/signed run_id receipt — the advertised 'verifiable audit receipt' is unverifiable on the MCP surface
findings.evidence: _triage returns TriageResponse(**result) (line 146); result from service.run_triage (service.py:129-135) contains only receipt (audit.receipt_line() — a plain human string, core/audit.py:261-273) and audit summary (self-reported counts). The MCP path never mints a run_id nor calls receipts.persist(); only api/app.py:_run does (127-129). TriageResponse.run_id defaults to None (schemas.py:72).
findings.rationale: Factually correct: MCP returns no signed/persisted/fetchable receipt, run_id always None, vs HTTP. But INSTRUCTIONS' 'verifiable audit receipt' refers to the independent audit summary, which IS returned; the missing signed/persisted artifact is a real but minor gap on a single surface — low.

findings.title: Non-rate-limit error from batch.execute() is unhandled and crashes the entire triage/summary/escalate run
findings.evidence: Line 248 `self._execute_with_backoff(batch.execute, "batch get")` has no surrounding try/except. _execute_with_backoff (lines 108-126) only retries 403/429 with rate-limit tags and re-raises any other HttpError. A 400/500/401 on the whole batch GET propagates up through batch_get_details into cmd_escalate/cmd_summary/cmd_pending (cli.py:867-868) and crashes the run. The per-message path catches 404 (line 182) but the batch path has no equivalent guard.
findings.rationale: A single non-rate-limit batch failure crashes the entire triage run; confirmed missing guard.

findings.location: apply_label() / remove_label() / star() / mark_read() etc., lines 277-283, 301-307, 328-344, 361-377
findings.title: IMAP X-GM-LABELS / FLAGS STORE operations return True without checking the IMAP response status
findings.evidence: Same unchecked-uid('STORE')-return defect across X-GM-LABELS and FLAGS methods; contrasts the checked COPY at 288. Verbatim the same root cause as U085 (which also lists archive 321).
findings.rationale: Identical to U085; severity upgraded to match the audited-trust blast radius.

findings.location: star line 331, unstar line 340, mark_read line 364, mark_unread line 373
findings.title: IMAP star/unstar/mark_read/mark_unread ignore STORE response and report success on server rejection
findings.evidence: star/unstar/mark_read/mark_unread (331/340/364/373) return True without checking the STORE tuple — same defect as U085. Correctly noted as lower blast radius since these do not move mail out of the inbox.
findings.rationale: Same root cause as U085, scoped to flag-only operations; medium severity is right for this subset.

findings.title: Tab/newline in sender or subject corrupts TSV parsing of osascript output
findings.evidence: Line 160 joins fields with literal "\t"; line 188 `parts = line.split("\t")`; line 189 `if len(parts) >= 5`. A subject containing a tab yields >5 parts and `parts[:5]` (line 190) misaligns is_read/is_flagged. A subject with a newline splits the row across `lines` (line 179). Subjects are arbitrary email content.
findings.rationale: Real delimiter-collision correctness bug; tab/newline in subject/sender silently mis-parses read/flagged flags. Not corroborated in slice but verifiable in code.

findings.title: Timeout/osascript failure in list_messages silently returns empty result, masking errors and ending pagination early
findings.evidence: _run_applescript raises RuntimeError on timeout (line 82) / non-zero exit (line 79). list_messages catches RuntimeError and returns ListMessagesResult(messages=[], next_page_token=None) (line 173-175). Empty result with no token is indistinguishable from 'no more messages' to the paginating caller.
findings.rationale: Confirmed silent-failure: a transient osascript failure ends pagination silently; sweep processes partial/no data with no error surfaced.

findings.title: OData $filter injection / breakage on folder names containing a single quote
findings.evidence: Line 642: `"$filter": f"displayName eq '{part}'"` interpolates `part` (a label segment from core/rules.py LABEL_RULES / config label_override) with no OData single-quote doubling.
findings.rationale: Confirmed unescaped OData interpolation; an apostrophe in a label breaks the filter. Downgraded to low: labels are developer/config-controlled (not attacker-controlled mail content), the broken filter is caught and only triggers RuntimeError, and OData string-literal injection here cannot escalate beyond filter scoping. Duplicate root cause with U225/U399.

findings.title: Read-modify-write of categories races and silently drops categories when the GET fails
findings.evidence: apply_category GETs categories (347), on Exception sets current_cats=[] (350), then if category not in current_cats appends and PATCHes {"categories": current_cats} (356-359). A failed GET therefore PATCHes [category] alone, overwriting any existing categories.
findings.rationale: Confirmed destructive-on-transient-read-failure plus non-atomic read-modify-write. Medium: requires the GET to fail at exactly the wrong moment; PATCH semantics are inherently overwrite (Graph has no add-to-collection). Duplicate of U400.

findings.title: Folder cache pagination not followed ($top 100 only) — mailboxes with >100 folders miss entries, causing duplicate folder creation
findings.evidence: Line 253 _api_get(GRAPH_API_FOLDERS, params={"$top": 100}) and line 268 childFolders also $top:100, neither follows @odata.nextLink (unlike list_messages which returns next_link at 469). Confirmed omission.
findings.rationale: Real omission, but >100 folders at a single level is uncommon for personal Outlook mailboxes; missing cache entries trigger create-then-fallback (which mostly fails safe). Low. corroborated=false echoed.

findings.title: No handling of HTTP 429 throttling / Retry-After; raise_for_status turns throttling into a hard error with no backoff
findings.evidence: All three helpers (216-218, 222-225, 229-232) call raise_for_status() with zero retry, no Retry-After honoring, no 429/503/504 handling. Gmail has _execute_with_backoff (per cross-file note) but Outlook has none.
findings.rationale: Confirmed absence of throttling resilience on a bulk-designed provider. Medium (not high) since the 1s/batch throttle in cli.py reduces 429 likelihood for typical runs. Read-path duplicate; U224 covers the write-path angle.

findings.title: Bare 'except: pass' silently produces an incomplete folder cache, causing duplicate folders / wrong moves
findings.evidence: Lines 273-274: `except Exception: pass  # Ignore errors for child folders` — swallows all errors (auth/429/network) with no log, leaving the folder cache silently incomplete.
findings.rationale: Confirmed bare-except-pass producing a silent incomplete cache. Low: downstream effect is create-then-find fallback which is largely self-healing; main harm is a missing log line. Duplicate of U402.

findings.title: Outlook due-date strftime hard-codes 'Z' (UTC) and zeroes the clock, mislabeling naive/local or non-UTC due_date at the wrong UTC date
findings.evidence: Line 574 does emit `due_date.strftime("%Y-%m-%dT00:00:00Z")` with timeZone UTC, ignoring tzinfo. BUT a repo-wide grep shows action.due_date is NEVER assigned anywhere in cli.py or core/ (only declared default None in models.py:93 and merged in :107). base.apply_actions:357 always calls star(due_date=action.due_date) = None. The `if due_date:` branch (572) is dead code.
findings.rationale: The strftime/tz bug is technically real but unreachable in the current codebase since no caller ever populates due_date — downgraded to info. corroborated=false echoed.

findings.location: _api_get/_api_post/_api_patch 213-232; no 429 Retry-After handling on mutation calls
findings.title: No 429/Retry-After backoff on apply_label/apply_category/star mutation calls — bulk tier-routing aborts each item on throttle with no retry
findings.evidence: Mutation helpers _api_post/_api_patch (220-232) have no 429/Retry-After/backoff; apply_label (532-537), apply_category (358-364), star (582-587) each catch the resulting HTTPError and return False with no retry. Tier-routing does up to 3 writes/message (move + category PATCH + flag PATCH per cli.py 252-264).
findings.rationale: Confirmed write-path throttle amplification distinct from U221's read angle: a throttled bulk run silently drops mutations and keeps hammering. Medium.

findings.title: OData injection in folder lookup ($filter displayName eq '{part}')
findings.evidence: Same line 642 `f"displayName eq '{part}'"` unescaped OData filter as U218.
findings.rationale: Same root cause as U218 (unescaped OData $filter interpolation). Duplicate.

findings.title: pyproject.toml has no [project] table / build backend / console_scripts — package is not installable
findings.evidence: pyproject.toml is exactly 3 lines: only [tool.pytest.ini_options] with testpaths and pythonpath. No [project], no [build-system], no requires-python, no [project.scripts]. setup.py and setup.cfg are absent (ls: No such file). mcp_server/server.py:154-155 defines main() with argparse.ArgumentParser(prog="universal-mail-mcp") but no console entry point declares it. NOTE: contrary to the finding, mcp_server/__main__.py DOES exist (148 bytes), so `python -m mcp_server` works from a checked-out tree.
findings.rationale: Factually accurate that the package is not pip-installable/distributable and the version floor is prose-only; but the finding's claim about `python -m mcp_server` requiring cwd-on-path is weak since __main__.py exists, and the deploy path (Docker uvicorn from /app) is acknowledged-benign, so real blast radius is low not medium.

findings.title: server.json advertises a PyPI package universal-mail-automation 0.1.0 that the repo cannot build (pyproject.toml has no [project] metadata)
findings.evidence: server.json:6-14 declares packages[0]={registryType:pypi, identifier:'universal-mail-automation', version:'0.1.0', transport stdio}. pyproject.toml has no [project] name/version/build-system, so this repo cannot build that pypi artifact. Cross-file claim is real. The name in server.json is `io.github.a-organvm/universal-mail-automation` and llms.txt:6 advertises `stdio: python -m mcp_server`.
findings.rationale: Real cross-file inconsistency (advertised pypi install surface is unbuildable from source), but severity is overstated: no such package is published on PyPI today, so the failure is an aspirational/manifest-drift issue, not an active broken install path. Distinct consequence from U226 so not a duplicate, but same missing-[project] root cause.

findings.location: lines 2-11 (all `>=` lower bounds, no upper caps); also requirements-api.txt:3-8 (fastapi>=0.110, uvicorn>=0.29, httpx>=0.27, pydantic>=2.0)
findings.title: Dependency floors are unpinned (>= only) with no lockfile — an upstream minor can break the build silently
findings.evidence: Confirmed: requirements.txt lines 2-11 are floor-only (google-api-python-client>=2.0.0, google-auth-httplib2>=0.1.0, google-auth-oauthlib>=0.5.0, msal>=1.25.0, requests>=2.28.0, pyyaml>=6.0). requirements-api.txt:3-8 floor-only (fastapi>=0.110, uvicorn[standard]>=0.29, httpx>=0.27, pydantic>=2.0). Only stripe (>=15.2,<16) and mcp (>=1.2,<2) are capped — and those caps live in requirements-api.txt:13 / requirements-mcp.txt, NOT in requirements.txt. No lockfile present (ls confirmed: no poetry.lock/Pipfile.lock/uv.lock/constraints.txt). CI: `pip install -r ...` with `cache: 'pip'`.
findings.rationale: All facts accurate, but 'break the build silently' is overstated — pytest is the documented hard gate that would surface most regressions; this is a reproducibility/supply-chain hygiene gap, not a latent crash.

findings.title: Domain match runs against full From string including display name — display-name spoofing routes legitimate mail
findings.evidence: Line 26 `set s to sender of msg`; line 9 tests `theSender contains ("@" & dom)` and `theSender ends with dom` against the whole string. providers/mailapp.py:150,190,193 reads `set msgSender to sender of msg` and assigns it directly to EmailMessage.sender with no address extraction, confirming `sender of msg` is the full From form ('Display Name <addr@domain>'). The Python gate instead parses via parseaddr (core/rules.py:746 docstring: 'RFC 5322-parses via parseaddr (the display NAME never feeds the gate)', line 755 `display, addr = parseaddr(decoded)`). The AppleScript does no such extraction, so `contains` can match display-name content (e.g. display name containing 'mailchimp.com').
findings.rationale: Confirmed: `sender of msg` carries display name + address, the `contains` test runs against the whole string, and the cited Python gate deliberately strips the display name — this script does not.

findings.location: module docstring lines 5-11 and main() lines 96-109 (writes pricing.md, llms.txt, .well-known/agent.json, server.json — but NOT acp/product_feed.json)
findings.title: Commerce-artifact generator does not regenerate acp/product_feed.json — the feed is on a second, separate generation path and can drift
findings.evidence: main() (lines 97-108) writes exactly pricing.md, llms.txt, .well-known/agent.json, server.json; product_feed.json is absent. acp/feed.py has a SEPARATE entrypoint `if __name__ == '__main__': write_static_feed()` (lines 80-85) that regenerates acp/product_feed.json. Both surfaces read api.plans.CREDIT_PACKS (gen_pricing_md line 61; acp/feed.py build_feed line 38). DEPLOY.md:79 documents ONLY `PUBLIC_BASE_URL=... python scripts/gen_commerce_artifacts.py` — no mention of `python -m acp.feed`.
findings.rationale: Two independent generation paths derive from the same plans.py catalog; the documented single command refreshes 3 of 4 commerce artifacts and silently leaves product_feed.json stale, exactly defeating the docstring's 'never drift' claim.

findings.title: Fake payment client ignores amount/currency/token/idempotency_key — charged amount never asserted
findings.evidence: tests/test_acp.py:26-27 _OKPay.charge returns a fixed ChargeResult(ok=True, payment_id='pi_test_123') ignoring amount/currency/token/idempotency_key. The success test asserts only run_credits==200 (line 133) and runs_credited==200 (line 138); grep for 'amount' in the test shows it appears ONLY in the fake's signature (line 26), never in an assertion. router.py:257 amount=grand_total(...) and router.py:305 places 'amount' in the receipt summary, so the value is observable but unasserted. Same root-cause defect as U234.
findings.rationale: Identical defect to U234 (fake charge ignores amount; charged amount never asserted) phrased less precisely; folding into U234.

findings.title: Test encodes the arbitrary-bearer-funding defect as intended behavior, locking in the broken identity model
findings.evidence: test asserts get_store().get_account_by_api_key('testkey')['run_credits']==200 (line 133, not 132) and 100 (line 153); line 162 create_account(api_key='testkey'). router.py:287-289 deliberately keys fulfillment by ctx.api_key (the parsed Bearer), creating the account on first use. So the FACTUAL claim (credits land on an account keyed by the caller-supplied bearer) is accurate. allow-secret
findings.rationale: The factual observation is correct, but 'broken identity model / auth-bypass anti-pattern' is an editorial judgment about an intentional router design choice, not a defect in this test file; severity inflated.

findings.location: whole file — no POST /acp/checkout_sessions/{id} update test (grep confirms only create/get/complete/cancel exercised)
findings.title: update_session (a price/items-mutating, money-relevant endpoint) has zero test coverage
findings.evidence: grep of tests/ for 'checkout_sessions/' shows only GET (lines 98, 100), /complete, and /cancel against {id}; no bare POST /acp/checkout_sessions/{sid}. router.py:200-232 update_session re-derives line_items/total_runs (217-225), guards invalid state (211-214), and re-prices on items=None via a two-source read (223-224) — none of this is exercised by any test.
findings.rationale: Grep confirms the money-mutating update endpoint has zero regression coverage; claim holds exactly as stated.

findings.title: Success-charge fake ignores the amount argument, so no test asserts the charged amount equals grand_total / equals the credited runs' price
findings.evidence: router.py:257 amount = models.grand_total(current['line_items']); router.py:262-266 passes that amount to charge(); the receipt summary carries it (router.py:305-306 'amount': amount). The test asserts run_credits==200 (line 133) and runs_credited==200 (line 138) but never 'amount'. Critically, pack_100 has amount_cents==100 AND runs==100 (api/plans.py:144), so the credited count and charged amount are numerically identical for this SKU — asserting one cannot pin the other. A per-run-vs-pack-total or qty-not-multiplied bug on the money axis would pass green.
findings.rationale: Confirmed money-path coverage gap: the only charged-amount evidence in the test is numerically aliased to runs because pack price==runs, so amount is genuinely unverified.

findings.title: Docstring claims 'real signature path' but every grant test monkeypatches construct_event away
findings.evidence: Confirmed mechanics: lines 71-72 and 106-107 do `monkeypatch.setattr(stripe.Webhook, "construct_event", lambda payload, sig, _whsec: event)`, so genuine HMAC verification over the raw body (billing.py:182 `stripe.Webhook.construct_event(payload, sig, webhook_secret)`) runs only in test_webhook_bad_signature_is_400 (44-49, asserts 400). The real-verification HMAC path is indeed never exercised on any GRANT path. BUT the docstring is not misleading: lines 3-4 explicitly state 'real signature path (bad signature -> 400) and the event-handling logic (via a monkeypatched construct_event)' — it discloses the monkeypatch outright.
findings.rationale: Real coverage gap (no fuzzing/forgery test on the grant path) but overstated: the docstring already discloses the monkeypatch, so the 'docstring claims X but really Y' framing is wrong; it is an honest, intentional unit-test seam.

findings.location: line 7 (import stripe) and line 14 (client = TestClient(app)); same module-level TestClient in test_api.py:10, test_acp.py:11, test_receipts.py:8, test_web.py:7
findings.title: Test modules construct TestClient(app) and import stripe at import time with no importorskip guard — pytest collection hard-crashes when optional API deps are absent
findings.evidence: Verified absent in this env: `import stripe` and `import fastapi` both raise ModuleNotFoundError. test_billing.py:7 `import stripe`, :8 `from fastapi.testclient import TestClient`, :14 `client = TestClient(app)` all run at collection time with no guard. Confirmed the 5 API modules construct `client = TestClient(app)` at module scope (test_api.py:10, test_acp.py:11, test_receipts.py:8, test_web.py:7) while test_mcp.py:11 uses `pytest.importorskip("mcp")`. So pytest collection of these modules ERRORs (not skips) on a partial install. NOTE: stripe/fastapi/pydantic/uvicorn ARE declared in requirements-api.txt, so this is NOT an undeclared-dependency defect — purely a partial-install test-robustness gap.
findings.rationale: Real and reproducible: collection ERROR vs SKIP on a deps-absent run; CI's full install masks it. Severity lowered to low — CI installs requirements-api.txt, and the failure mode is only the ergonomic 'core-only test run' scenario, no production blast radius.

findings.title: Tests exercise consume_credit/add_credits in isolation, masking that no production path calls them
findings.evidence: grep --include=*.py for consume_credit returns ONLY tests/test_store.py:39,42 plus the def at api/store.py:235 — zero production callers. add_credits is called only at tests/test_store.py:38 plus its def. No production route debits run_credits: grep for 'run_credits = run_credits -' / consume_credit / debit in api/ hits only store.py's consume_credit body; api/service.py and api/app.py contain no credit/consume/quota/cap logic. api/plans.py:189-192 (entitlements_for) merely *exposes* monthly_run_cap and run_credits but enforces nothing. grep of tests/ for monthly_run_cap|entitlements_for|429|quota = no hits. test_acp.py:133,153,173 do assert run_credits after ACP fulfill (via fulfill_once at store.py:384, a separate credit path), confirming only the credit-grant side is covered, never a debit.
findings.rationale: consume_credit/add_credits have no production callers; metering debit is never enforced or integration-tested, so the green unit tests mask a missing end-to-end metering path exactly as claimed.

findings.title: Pricing displayed on the web app diverges from the source-of-truth plans.py ($19/mo Pro, $49/mo Business) — customers see wrong prices
findings.evidence: SAMPLE_PLANS (web/index.html:689-702) hardcodes pro '$29-$49' / 'Inbox safety audit' and business '$149-$299' / 'Assisted cleanup'. plans.py (the documented single source of truth; its docstring at lines 3-6 explicitly warns against price drift 'across code, docs, and the website') sets Pro '$19/mo' and Business '$49/mo' (plans.py:87-125). worker.mjs likewise serves $19/$49 (worker.mjs:26,44). The website fallback values match NEITHER, so the source divergence is a real consistency defect.
findings.rationale: Genuine source-of-truth divergence the docstring explicitly warns about; downgraded to low because it only manifests in the unused JS fallback path, not on the live worker-served page.

findings.location: static sample table line 522-525 (founder@client.example -> 'Stays') vs localSenderCheck lines 751-762
findings.title: Static sample-audit table disagrees with the live sender-check tool on the same demo senders
findings.evidence: Sample table shows founder@client.example as 'Stays' (lines 509-511) and admin@new-domain.example / billing@unknown-vendor.example as 'Needs review' (lines 528-536). localSenderCheck would classify founder@client.example as protected:false ('Can move', line 753) and has no 'needs review' state. True for the offline fallback. BUT the live tool calls the worker (worker.mjs senderCheck), and worker ALSO returns protected:false for founder@client.example (line 153 only protects chase/1password/.gov), so the contradiction is identical on the live demo — it is not specific to the offline fallback. Either way 'needs review' is absent from both the tool and the worker.
findings.rationale: The table-vs-tool inconsistency is real, but it is a curated-demo vs simplistic-classifier mismatch present on the live worker too; cosmetic credibility nit, not a fallback-only bug as framed.

findings.title: Smoke test runs immediately after wrangler deploy with no propagation wait — flaky failures
findings.evidence: ci.yml:108-119 'Smoke test live share demo' runs in the same job immediately after the 'Deploy Worker' step (lines 100-106), under `set -euo pipefail` (line 111) with `curl -fsS` (lines 113-119) and no `--retry`, sleep, or poll loop. Edge/route propagation lag would fail the job.
findings.rationale: Real flakiness risk, but bounded: only fires on push to main with a configured token, and a red post-deploy smoke is an alarm not a corruption. Severity low. Substantially overlaps U485/U437 but is a distinct id from a different round; not marking duplicate since these are sibling findings, not same-file dupes per instructions.

findings.location: line 30 (single combined `pip install -r requirements.txt -r requirements-api.txt -r requirements-mcp.txt`)
findings.title: The 3.9-isolation contract is undermined by always installing requirements-mcp.txt in CI
findings.evidence: ci.yml:30 always installs all three requirements files on every matrix interpreter. requirements-mcp.txt:1-6 documents the ISOLATED contract ('Install only where you run the MCP server') and tests/test_mcp.py:11 uses pytest.importorskip('mcp') precisely as the 3.9-safety net. CI never exercises a core-only (requirements.txt only) install path, so the advertised 'core importable on 3.9 without mcp' is unvalidated.
findings.rationale: Real but lower-impact: the isolation contract is genuinely never exercised, but since 3.9 isn't in the matrix the combined install does not currently break anything. Severity low (matches the architecture-honesty concern, not a live failure).

findings.location: feed (lines 67-69) -> build_feed (33-64); also router.py _links (125-130) and permalink_url (321)
findings.title: Host header reflected into absolute feed/checkout/seller URLs (Host-header injection / cache-poisoning of the agent discovery surface)
findings.evidence: feed.py:69 `JSONResponse(build_feed(str(request.base_url)))`; build_feed embeds base into url/image_url (45-47), seller_url (55), checkout_url (62). request.base_url is derived from the client Host header in Starlette. router.py:126 `_links` and :321 `permalink_url` also build from request.base_url. No env-configured trusted origin is used at request time.
findings.rationale: Genuine Host-header reflection into the agent-facing checkout/seller URLs; exploitability depends on a poisonable shared cache or a proxy that trusts client Host, so medium (not high) is right.

findings.location: build_line_items lines 86-96 (base = int(pack['amount_cents']); subtotal = base * qty; total_runs += int(pack['runs']) * qty)
findings.title: Line-item subtotal and total_runs are unbounded int*qty products with no per-session amount cap before charging
findings.evidence: models.py:87 subtotal=base*qty, :96 total_runs+=runs*qty. Item.quantity is bounded le=1000 (line 27) but CheckoutCreate.items (line 43) has no max_items. router.py:257 amount=grand_total(current['line_items']) is passed verbatim to payment.get_payment_client().charge(amount=...) (router.py:262-266). No monetary ceiling exists in models.py, router.py, or payment.py. payment.py:89-91 returns generic 'charge failed' on Stripe rejection.
findings.rationale: Real: there is genuinely no aggregate amount cap before the charge, and Stripe rejection surfaces as a generic error — but blast radius is limited (auth-gated endpoint, fail-closed charge, Stripe enforces its own ceiling), so medium is inflated.

findings.location: grand_total lines 100-101 and build_line_items lines 86-94 (base_amount/subtotal/total all integer cents); build_totals 104-111
findings.title: grand_total sums line_items 'total' which equals subtotal (tax always 0) — totals math diverges from build_totals and bakes in a no-tax assumption on the charged amount
findings.evidence: models.py:92-94 per-line total=subtotal with tax hard-coded 0; grand_total (100-101) sums li['total']. build_totals (104-111) independently computes total=sub+tax. router.py:257 charges amount=grand_total(current['line_items']) — the line-item path, NOT build_totals. The two paths are independent summations on the same session.
findings.rationale: Real latent divergence: two independent money-summation paths agree only because tax is constant 0; if tax is ever added to build_totals the charged amount would diverge from the displayed total. Currently dormant, so low not medium.

findings.location: Item.quantity Field le=1000 line 27 + build_line_items line 96 (total_runs += runs*qty); plans.CREDIT_PACKS pack_1000 line 146-147
findings.title: Single ACP session can legitimately bill ~$9,000 / credit 1,000,000 runs with no high-value confirmation or per-session cap
findings.evidence: models.py:27 quantity le=1000; line 43 items unbounded. plans.py:146-147 pack_1000 = {runs:1000, amount_cents:900}. One item {pack_1000, qty 1000} => total_runs=1,000,000, subtotal=900*1000=900,000 cents=$9,000 (models.py:87,96). No per-session ceiling and no high-value confirmation before charge (router.py:262). Math verified against the real catalog.
findings.rationale: Arithmetic and absence-of-cap are confirmed against real code/catalog, but this is the same root cause as U243 (no aggregate cap); attacker still needs a valid auth bearer + a chargeable SPT, so medium overstates real risk.

findings.location: build_line_items line 79 (valid = bool(items)) + router.py create line 176/188 (_persist with total_runs)
findings.title: Empty-items create yields valid=False but is still persisted; downstream relies solely on status guard, not on amount/runs being zero
findings.evidence: models.py:79 valid=bool(items); empty list => valid=False, total_runs=0, line_items=[]. router.py:176 status=STATUS_NOT_READY when invalid; persisted at :188. complete_session (router.py:252) refuses any non-READY status. There is no amount>0/total_runs>0 assertion on the charge/fulfill path — the only guard is the status string check. grand_total([])=0 (models.py:101).
findings.rationale: Accurate description of current behavior: the zero-amount/zero-runs invariant is enforced only via the status string, with no defense-in-depth amount>0 check. The exploit is purely hypothetical (requires a separate future bug to flip status), so low.

findings.title: ACP charge swallows all exceptions into a generic 'charge failed', masking declines vs network vs config errors and any settlement ambiguity
findings.evidence: payment.py:89-91 is `except Exception as e: logger.warning(...); return ChargeResult(ok=False, error='charge failed')` — collapses declines, network timeouts, idempotency conflicts, config errors into one opaque result. The per-session idempotency_key=f'acp-charge:{session_id}' (router.py:265) is the only protection against a network-timeout-after-create double charge.
findings.rationale: Real but overstated as the ambiguous-settlement hazard: the per-session idempotency key genuinely dedups the retry to the same intent, and there is no caller branch on error category. Confirmed as an error-handling coarseness defect; severity low (no money loss, retry-safe via idempotency).

findings.location: 67-97 (no idempotent re-read of an existing intent)
findings.title: Charge retry under same idempotency_key returns Stripe's replayed intent but never reconciles a previously-succeeded-then-uncredited charge
findings.evidence: charge() has no branch distinguishing a Stripe-replayed already-succeeded intent from a fresh success (returns ok=True with payment_id either way). router.py:290 fulfill_once is the sole guard; receipt save (313-317) and _persist (337) happen AFTER fulfill_once commits. If a crash lands between fulfill_once and save_receipt, the retry re-charges (replayed, ok), fulfill_once returns False, and the elif at 323-330 yields a 200 with order=None (no receipt minted). The docstring 'crash-retry-safe' (router 282-285) is thus false for that window.
findings.rationale: Confirmed window exists: post-fulfill/pre-receipt crash yields a charged session with no receipt. Severity low: the runs ARE credited (fulfill_once committed), only the signed receipt is missing; the buyer is not defrauded, the audit artifact is.

findings.title: Re-completing an already-completed session via a fresh Idempotency-Key skips charge but the early-return path also stores the completed response under the new key without re-validating ownership
findings.evidence: When current['status'] == STATUS_COMPLETED, complete_session returns `current` and calls _complete_idempotency(ctx, current) (248-251), storing the prior buyer's completed session (order/permalink/buyer email) under the NEW caller's idempotency key. Combined with no api_key binding (_gate 79-83, no ownership in _load), a second party with any bearer + known session id receives the first buyer's full completed session.
findings.rationale: Confirmed disclosure + semantically-wrong cross-key persistence. Shares the no-ownership-binding root with U014/U017 but adds the completed-replay-storage angle; kept distinct.

findings.title: On idempotent re-completion where fulfilled=False and a prior order exists, no order is re-attached but response still reports status completed — order may be dropped from the response
findings.evidence: order = current.get('order') (293). If fulfill_once returns False AND current has no stored order, the elif at 323 emits an info message but leaves order=None, so the final response (332-336) carries no order/permalink. This occurs only if the FIRST /complete crashed AFTER fulfill_once committed (290) but BEFORE _persist saved the order (318-337). The first attempt's random order_id (296) is unrecoverable.
findings.rationale: Real durability gap for the crash-after-credit case. This is the same crash window as U018; U018 frames it as receipt-loss-with-2xx, U250 as order-dropped-from-response. Same root crash-between-290-and-337.

findings.title: Bearer credential length is unbounded and unvalidated; arbitrary attacker-chosen strings become account api_keys
findings.evidence: _gate sets api_key = auth[len('Bearer '):].strip() with no length/charset/format check (79-83); complete_session inserts it into accounts.api_key (UNIQUE, store.py:50) via create_account (289). No bound, unlike pydantic billing/model fields. allow-secret
findings.rationale: Accurate but the lowest-impact slice of the unvalidated-bearer cluster (length/charset). Downgraded to low; root cause is U016.

findings.title: ACP /complete auto-creates an account from the bearer token, so any unauthenticated bearer can mint an account and be credited paid runs after a (configured) charge
findings.evidence: Same code as U016/U013: get_account_by_api_key then create_account(api_key=ctx.api_key) on miss (287-289). Note: with NullPaymentClient (default, no STRIPE_SECRET_KEY) result.ok is False (payment.py:55-56) so no fulfillment — the exploit requires Stripe SPT configured. allow-secret
findings.rationale: Identical auto-provision root cause to U016; correctly flags that the default Null client gates the immediate exploit.

findings.title: Failed-charge 402 response is cached under the Idempotency-Key, locking out retries with that key
findings.evidence: On a failed charge the handler builds a 402 resp and calls _complete_idempotency(ctx, resp) at 279, which sets the idempotency key status='done' with that 402 body (store.py:355-362). A retry reusing the same Idempotency-Key hits _begin_idempotency -> state 'replay' -> returns the cached 402 verbatim with Idempotent-Replayed: true (router.py:171-172/240-242, store.py:350-353) and never re-attempts the charge.
findings.rationale: Confirmed: a transient decline locks that key to 402; client must mint a fresh key. Conversion footgun, no money loss. Medium fair.

findings.title: Charge happens before account resolution; a post-charge failure path credits nothing but already captured funds
findings.evidence: Charge at 262-266 precedes account resolution; if get_account_by_api_key/create_account raises (DB error, or a UNIQUE api_key collision from a concurrent create) the exception propagates after funds captured and fulfill_once never runs — no credit, no receipt, no compensation. No try/except around 286-290.
findings.rationale: Real charge-then-fail window. Same uncovered post-charge region as U110, but U110 covers the full 286-338 block (including receipt/persist) while U254 isolates the account-resolution sub-window; same root (no transaction/compensation around post-charge work).

findings.title: Idempotency-Key is global (PRIMARY KEY on key alone), not scoped per api_key — cross-tenant key collision can leak another caller's response
findings.evidence: store.py:73-74 `idempotency_keys (key TEXT PRIMARY KEY, ...)` — key is the sole PK. idempotency_begin (store.py:322-325) looks up `WHERE key = ?` only; scope/request_hash are stored but NOT part of the uniqueness or the dedup match for replay (lines 348-353 return the stored response on any same-hash same-key hit regardless of principal). _gate (router.py:72-93) never namespaces the Idempotency-Key by api_key. A second caller using the same client-chosen key + identical body + same scope receives the first caller's stored response_json — which for acp.complete (332-339) includes buyer/order/permalink.
findings.rationale: Idempotency uniqueness is global on the client-chosen key with no principal namespacing; same-body cross-tenant collision yields another caller's response. Real but requires an identical body and a predictable/colliding key, so medium holds.

findings.title: TOCTOU race between get_account_by_api_key and create_account can raise an unhandled IntegrityError (500)
findings.evidence: router.py:287-289 does get_account_by_api_key then `if account is None: create_account(...)` outside any shared transaction. store.py:50 `api_key TEXT UNIQUE`; create_account (store.py:159-163) is a bare INSERT with no OR IGNORE/ON CONFLICT and no try/except. Two concurrent /complete with the same fresh bearer both see None; the loser's INSERT raises sqlite3.IntegrityError, uncaught here, surfacing as a 500 rather than the ACPError envelope.
findings.rationale: TOCTOU is real and the INSERT has no conflict handling; concurrency is needed to trigger, so medium is appropriate.

findings.title: Buyer is charged before the account is resolved/created and before fulfill_once — a non-fulfilling state path can charge money without minting a retrievable order
findings.evidence: Charge at router.py:262-266 runs before get_account_by_api_key/create_account (287-289), save_receipt (313-317) and _persist (337). If create_account raises the U256 IntegrityError, the 500 fires AFTER the charge succeeded at Stripe; save_receipt is never reached, leaving charged-but-unrecorded. The per-session charge key (265) protects a *retry from the same session*, but the request itself returns 500 with no order minted.
findings.rationale: Ordering confirmed against source; charge precedes account resolution and any failure between them leaves money captured without a persisted order. Conditional on the U256 race, so medium.

findings.title: Account auto-provisioned at /complete is keyed only by api_key with no email/customer linkage, so ACP-purchased credits are orphaned from the Stripe-billing identity
findings.evidence: router.py:289 create_account(api_key=ctx.api_key, plan='free') — no email/customer. billing.py _resolve_account (208-219) resolves only by account_id (client_reference_id, line 226) or stripe_customer_id (get_account_by_customer), never by api_key. The two identity spaces are disjoint: get_account_by_api_key (store.py:170-175) vs get_account_by_customer (177-180). A buyer with an existing billing account who pays via ACP under an unseen bearer gets a second orphan account holding the run_credits. allow-secret
findings.rationale: Identity-key divergence between ACP (api_key) and billing (customer_id/client_reference_id) confirmed in source; credits can land on an orphan account invisible to the billing identity.

findings.location: complete_session lines 257, 262-266, 304-310 (amount = grand_total; receipt summary 'amount': amount + 'currency': CURRENCY)
findings.title: Charged amount is taken from the stored session line_items, which an update_session call can re-price between create and complete with no re-binding to the original quoted price
findings.evidence: amount = models.grand_total(current['line_items']) (router.py:257) reads the CURRENT stored line_items. update_session (217-225) re-prices in place when items supplied while status==READY and re-persists. The Stripe charge key is acp-charge:{session_id} (265), so a re-completed/retried session reuses the first charge amount at Stripe, while the receipt summary 'amount'/'runs_credited' (304-310) and credited total_runs (290) come from the current re-priced session. CREDIT_PACKS pack_100=100c, pack_1000=900c (plans.py:144-146) — distinct prices, so a create-at-100 then update-to-1000 is a concrete divergence path.
findings.rationale: Re-price-between-create-and-complete with a session-scoped charge key is a real divergence between settled amount and credited/receipted amount. Requires a multi-step update+retry sequence, so medium.

findings.location: complete_session lines 257-266 (amount = grand_total(...); charge(amount=amount)) — no amount > 0 guard before charging
findings.title: No positive-amount guard before charging: a READY session whose line_items sum to 0 charges/fulfills $0 (deeper instance — a future zero-priced or mis-edited pack)
findings.evidence: No `amount > 0` check before payment.charge (router.py:257-266). But build_line_items sets valid only on known-pack match (models.py:79-84) and all current CREDIT_PACKS have positive amount_cents (plans.py:144-146: 100, 900). A zero amount requires a future/edited zero-priced pack. StripeSPTPaymentClient sends int(0) which Stripe rejects (payment.py:67-97); only NullPaymentClient/test clients could 'succeed', and NullPaymentClient always returns ok=False (payment.py:55-56), so no real processor fulfills $0 today.
findings.rationale: Real defensive gap but only exploitable under a hypothetical zero-priced catalog edit; no current code path charges/fulfills $0. Severity downgraded to low.

findings.title: Payment-failed re-persist drops account_id binding via INSERT OR REPLACE
findings.evidence: Payment-failed branch _persist(session_id, resp, total_runs) (router.py:278) omits account_id, so _persist (149-155) calls save_session with account_id=None. save_session (store.py:407-412) is INSERT OR REPLACE on the full row, writing account_id=NULL. update_session (230) and cancel_session (357) also omit account_id. So any later failed-complete/update/cancel nulls a prior account_id binding on acp_sessions.
findings.rationale: Confirmed: partial _persist signature + full-row REPLACE nulls account_id. Impact is reconciliation/audit only (the durable fulfillment link lives in acp_fulfillments, store.py:92-97), so low.

findings.title: Stripe charge idempotency keyed on session_id, but fulfill dedup also keyed on session_id — an update that re-prices then re-completes cannot re-charge the delta
findings.evidence: Same root cause as U259: charge key acp-charge:{session_id} (265) + fulfill_once keyed on session_id (store.py:365-389) with no amount component means an update-repriced session reuses the first charge amount while credits/receipt reflect the new snapshot. router.py:257-290 inspected; the mechanism is identical to U259.
findings.rationale: Restates the U259 amount/session-key divergence from the fulfill-dedup angle; same defect.

findings.title: Re-completing a COMPLETED session under a NEW Idempotency-Key returns the stored completed response but never charges — yet a session marked completed with fulfilled=False (credit-applied-but-not-persisted) is indistinguishable
findings.evidence: STATUS_COMPLETED is persisted only at router.py:337 (_persist), AFTER fulfill_once (290) and save_receipt (313-317). The completion is a multi-write, non-transactional sequence (charge → fulfill_once → save_receipt → save_session), each its own store.py commit. A crash after fulfill_once but before _persist leaves the row at its last persisted status (READY), so the 248-251 early-return does not fire and a retry re-enters the charge path (charge dedups via Stripe key, fulfill_once returns False → 'already_fulfilled' branch 323-330, no order minted). Status is therefore not a sound completion sentinel.
findings.rationale: The non-atomic write order is confirmed in source; status-as-sentinel is unsound across a mid-sequence crash. Crash-window only, so medium.

findings.title: create_account at /complete uses account['id'] without handling a concurrent-insert IntegrityError or None return
findings.evidence: Same defect as U256: create_account (store.py:159-163) bare INSERT, accounts.api_key UNIQUE (store.py:50), no OR IGNORE/retry; concurrent /complete races raise an uncaught IntegrityError. account['id'] dereferenced at 290/337. The 'NEW specific' (no duplicate-handling in create_account) is the same root cause as U256's TOCTOU/IntegrityError.
findings.rationale: Adds the post-charge-money detail but is the same get-or-create race already filed as U256.

findings.location: complete_session line 257 vs acp/models.py build_totals line 104-111 / build_session line 138
findings.title: Buyer-displayed totals.total and the amount actually charged are derived from two different code paths and never compared
findings.evidence: Charge uses grand_total = sum(li['total']) (models.py:100-101). Buyer-facing totals.total = sum(subtotal)+sum(tax) (build_totals, models.py:104-111), surfaced via build_session line 138. Per line item, total==subtotal and tax=0 (build_line_items, models.py:88-95), so the two sums are provably equal today. No assertion ties them together.
findings.rationale: The two summations are distinct code paths but mathematically identical under the current line-item shape (tax always 0, total==subtotal). Divergence requires a future tax/discount feature, so this is a latent/defensive concern — low.

findings.location: update_session lines 217-225 (else branch keeps current['line_items'] but pulls total_runs from row['data']['total_runs'])
findings.title: update_session without items reads line_items and total_runs from two different stored locations, then re-shapes — desync entry point
findings.evidence: router.py:223-224: when body.items is None, line_items=current['line_items'] (from data['response']) and total_runs=row['data']['total_runs'] (sibling top-level field). They are written together by _persist (149-155, data={'response':resp,'total_runs':...}) at every create/update, so they agree at write time. No re-derivation against CREDIT_PACKS occurs on the buyer-only update.
findings.rationale: Two persisted sources read separately is real, but they are always written atomically together so no desync exists today; only a future partial-write bug would break it. Latent — low.

findings.location: complete_session line 290 fulfill_once + lines 295-317 order minting (order_id minted only when fulfilled=True, but charge already succeeded)
findings.title: Charge succeeds then fulfill_once True but save_receipt/save_session can raise, leaving funds captured with no retry path that re-mints the order
findings.evidence: After charge ok (267) and fulfill_once True (290), save_receipt (313-317) and _persist (337) follow. fulfill_once (store.py:365-389) commits the credit + acp_fulfillments row independently of the later receipt write. If save_receipt or _persist raises, the request 500s with money captured and credits applied; a retry finds fulfilled=False (290) and takes the 'already_fulfilled' branch (323-330) returning completed with order=None and no receipt — the signed receipt is never re-minted.
findings.rationale: The order/receipt mint is gated on fulfilled=True which is consumed by the first (failed-after-fulfill) attempt; the verifiable receipt is irrecoverable on that path. Crash/DB-error window, so medium.

findings.location: lines 38-45 (try/except around `from mcp_server.server import http_app, mcp`)
findings.title: MCP import failure (including a missing/renamed `mcp` internal symbol) silently downgrades a paid `/mcp` endpoint to 404 at INFO log level
findings.evidence: app.py:38-45 wraps the import in `except Exception` and logs at logger.info (line 45); a failure sets _MCP_AVAILABLE=False and the /mcp mount at line 143 is skipped. mcp_server/server.py:32 does import the internal path `from mcp.server.streamable_http import TransportSecuritySettings`, and requirements-mcp.txt pins `mcp>=1.2,<2` (wide 1.x range). So the fragile-internal-path + INFO-only-degrade mechanism is real. However the finding's parenthetical 'missing/renamed `mcp` internal symbol' conflates this with app.py:39's `mcp as _mcp`, which is mcp_server.server's OWN FastMCP instance (server.py:73), not an SDK symbol.
findings.rationale: Mechanism is real but overstated: it is a graceful-degradation design choice (intentional 3.9 fallback) logged at INFO, not a silent bug on the core paid surface; downgrade to low.

findings.title: persist() always called with account_id=None on the public triage path, so every triage receipt is unattributed and list_receipts can never return them
findings.evidence: _run calls receipts.persist(run_id, result) with no account_id (app.py:129); persist defaults None (receipts.py:88-89); save_receipt stores account_id NULL (store.py:269-298). list_receipts DOES filter WHERE account_id = ? (store.py:300-306). BUT list_receipts has ZERO callers anywhere (grep across api/acp/mcp_server) — no hosted-receipt-history endpoint exists. The audit endpoint get_receipt (receipts.py:114-142) fetches by run_id and does NOT filter by account, so receipts ARE individually retrievable.
findings.rationale: Attribution gap (NULL account_id) is real, but the consequence is overstated: the 'hosted-receipt-history feature can never surface receipts' is moot because that feature is not wired (list_receipts unused), and per-run audit retrieval works. Low.

findings.title: Webhook references stripe.error.SignatureVerificationError without guaranteeing the attribute exists across Stripe SDK major versions
findings.evidence: requirements-api.txt:13 pins `stripe>=15.2,<16`. Installed venv is stripe 15.2.0.
