findings.title: remove_label always returns False, so base.apply_actions never registers INBOX-removal as leaving the inbox for Outlook
findings.evidence: remove_label (539-542) is a hard no-op returning False. base.apply_actions lines 350-352 gate did_leave_inbox on `self.remove_label(...) and label.upper() in ('INBOX','\\INBOX')`, so an INBOX in remove_labels never marks departure for Outlook. But Outlook sets LABEL_IS_MOVE=True (line 102) and apply_label issues a Graph /move (529-533); base.py 347-348 sets did_label_move, and 377-378 computes moved = did_leave_inbox or did_label_move. Departure is correctly captured via the move path, and the audit comment at 74 documents this design.
findings.rationale: The factual claim (remove_label always False) is true, but the consequence is overstated: this is the documented intentional folder/move design, not a latent audit gap. Real-but-overstated -> partial, info.

findings.title: Optional deps (msal/requests/pyyaml) listed as hard requirements despite being documented optional
findings.evidence: msal/requests are listed unconditionally under '# Optional: Outlook.com support' (l6-8) and pyyaml under '# Optional: YAML configuration file support' (l10-11), so `pip install -r requirements.txt` always pulls them. Yet they are lazy-imported in code: msal at providers/outlook.py:138, requests at providers/outlook.py:202, yaml at core/config.py:111. The 'Optional' comment contradicts the unconditional install line.
findings.rationale: Confirmed clarity/footprint inconsistency (comment says optional, install line is unconditional, code defers import); cosmetic, correctly rated info.

findings.title: iCloud IMAP password passed via inline env assignment on the command (process-listing exposure)
findings.evidence: Identical root cause and identical location (lines 47-50): IMAP_PASS passed via inline env-prefix assignment, flagged as readable via /proc/<pid>/environ. Same defect as U864, same overstated /proc mechanism (no /proc on Darwin).
findings.rationale: Same secret-in-child-env observation as U864 at the same lines; collapse to a single finding.

findings.title: Generated files written non-atomically; a crash mid-write leaves committed artifacts truncated
findings.evidence: Each of the four artifacts opens its final path directly with open(path, 'w') (lines 97, 99, 103, 106); no temp-file + os.replace. A raise between writes leaves a partially-regenerated set.
findings.rationale: Technically accurate but a dev-time generator producing git-tracked files reviewed before commit; a crash yields a visible dirty diff, not a shipped truncated artifact — info severity is correct, no human action warranted.

findings.title: Money formatting uses float division for cents -> dollars (rounding risk)
findings.evidence: Identical claim and identical location as U660: float division ${pk['amount_cents'] / 100:.2f} at gen_pricing_md lines 62-63. Same proposed fix (f"{amt//100}.{amt%100:02d}").
findings.rationale: Same root-cause money-formatting finding as U660 in the same file at the same lines; folded as a duplicate.

findings.title: Triage tests stub run_labeler so the real audit chokepoint wiring inside run_labeler is bypassed
findings.evidence: Both tests monkeypatch service.run_labeler with `_fake_run_labeler` that directly calls audit.record(...) with hand-chosen protected/archived flags (test_api.py:88-99 records protected=False,archived=True; lines 110-124 record protected=True/archived=False), bypassing the real run_labeler->apply_actions->audit.record path. That real path IS covered in tests/test_audit.py: TestRunLabelerReceipt (line 370, `from cli import run_labeler` at 375, real run_labeler at 380) and TestChokepointIntegration (line 191) exercising apply_actions->audit.record across providers.
findings.rationale: Accurate observation about test scoping; finding self-identifies as 'Note rather than a defect' and the real integration is genuinely covered elsewhere, so info severity is correct.

findings.title: Redirect test accepts any 3xx and only checks Location header, not that the dashboard is actually reachable via the redirect
findings.evidence: tests/test_web.py:18-20 asserts `r.status_code in (301, 302, 307, 308)` and `r.headers['location'] == '/app/'` with `follow_redirects=False`, never following through to confirm /app/ returns 200 in the same flow. api/app.py:159-160 returns `RedirectResponse(url='/app/')` (FastAPI default 307), so a regression changing the status code to e.g. 301 would still pass the broad tuple assertion.
findings.rationale: Accurate test-quality observation: the assertion accepts any of four 3xx codes and only checks the Location header, so it cannot detect a semantically-different-but-still-3xx redirect; correctly self-scoped as info severity, not a runtime defect.

findings.title: Sender/subject sent to API with no client-side length bound
findings.evidence: Line 807 posts `{ sender, subject: $("subject").value }` with no client length cap — true. But the claim that 'SenderCheckRequest's subject/sender bounds are not enforced' is wrong: schemas.py:29-30 sets sender = Field(max_length=4096) and subject = Field(default="", max_length=4096), so the FastAPI backend rejects oversized input (422). The worker reads body.sender without a length issue (pure string ops). The authoritative server bound exists.
findings.rationale: The premise that the server lacks sender/subject bounds is false — schemas.py enforces max_length=4096 on both; the client simply lacks a redundant pre-check, which is immaterial.

downstream_guidance.known_false_positive_class: Round-2 findings claiming FastAPI/uvicorn/pydantic/stripe/mcp are 'undeclared dependencies' are FALSE POSITIVES — they are declared in requirements-api.txt and requirements-mcp.txt (confirmed by R3 deploy-config finder). Drop these in ranking.
downstream_guidance.false_concern_corrected: R3 'protected_senders.local.txt is git-tracked' concern is FALSE — it is correctly .gitignored and never committed. The REAL committed-data leak is labeler_state.json (tracked, public, 36470-email counts).
downstream_guidance.scope_correction: create_smart_mailboxes.scpt is plaintext AppleScript (not a compiled binary as R1/R2 assumed) — reviewed in R4.
downstream_guidance.clustering_note: Raw counts include heavy semantic clustering (e.g. the unauth+no-metering money-path defect was reported ~15x across finders/rounds). cross_round_corroborated finms (rounds>1) and same-file/same-line groups should be collapsed before triage; high corroboration = high signal.
downstream_guidance.verification_target: Highest-leverage entries are the structural_gaps (whole-subsystem logic gaps), not individual line findings.
downstream_guidance.corroboration_signal: 427 findings carry cross_round_line_corroborated=true (a finding from a DIFFERENT round flags the same file + overlapping lines). Treat these as higher-confidence: independent finders re-discovered them. Only 0 were EXACT (file+line+title) duplicates — semantic re-discovery dominates, so cluster by file+line, not by string match.
